Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Agencies Cannot Reach IDPs Trapped By Fighting
Body_Text:
Goma — Tens of thousands of civilians forced from their homes by fresh fighting in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo are still beyond the reach of international aid, two weeks after the launch of a rebel offensive.
Swathes of countryside previously safe for humanitarian operations are now under the control of renegade general, Laurent Nkunda, head of the rebel Congrès National pour la Défense du Peuple (CNDP).

Original_Search_Title: NGO Accuses All Forces of Rape, Brutality
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Increase in Forced Labour, Rape and Harassment in East, Says Oxfam
Body_Text:
There has been an increase in incidents of forced labour, rape and widespread brutality, according to assessments carried out by international agency Oxfam over the past week, as armed men from all sides prey upon those who have sought 'sanctuary' from the fighting in North Kivu.
Oxfam visited camps and communities in Kibati, just north of Goma; Sake and Minova, south of Goma; and Kanyabayonga, north of Rutshuru. Findings reveal that civilians continue to face widespread brutality after they have fled from the heart of the fighting.

Original_Search_Title: Lokpobiri and the Shameful Defence of N37 Billion LOC Budget
Headline: Nigeria: Lokpobiri and the Shameful Defence of N37 Billion LOC Budget
Body_Text:
Events which happen in this country at times make us look stupid before people of other climes as members of our National Assembly, particularly the upper house, the Senate, always want to see themselves as superior to other Nigerians.
They never, at any time, see themselves as mere representatives of the people but always try to impress it on us all that they are doing us a great favour by being in the National Assembly.

Original_Search_Title: 37 Billion Budget - Lokpobiri, Adamu's Comments Affront On Yar Adua
Headline: Nigeria: 37 Billion Budget - Lokpobiri, Adamu's Comments Affront On Yar Adua
Body_Text:
Last Monday at the Senate, the Committee on Sports led by Senator Heineken Lokpobiri took on the LOC big wigs and the Director-General of the National Sports Commission (NSC), Dr. Amos Adamu in what seemed like an image-laundering exercise and virtually condemned President Umar Musa Yar'Adua's decision to scale down the bogus and outrageous N37 billion drawn by the LOC for the U-17 World Cup which the country will host next year.
While Senator Lokpobiri said Nigerians were crying wolf over "a paltry N37 billion" and that the money would not even be enough for the championship which is the least among the FIFA competitions, Dr Adamu said the President was "deceived" by the former Minister of Sports and Chairman, NSC, Barrister Abdulrahman Gimba into taking "a wrong decision" of either first canceling the hosting of the event or slashing the budget.

Original_Search_Title: Wireless Internet in the Countryside
Headline: Africa: Wireless Internet in the Countryside
Body_Text:
Washington, DC — Two case studies in Tanzania, discussed in a new report by wireless internet expert Ian Howard for the Association for Progressive Communications, show  two very different models for building sustainable telecentres to meet needs in rural areas.  The Family Alliance for Development and Cooperation is an initiative by self-taught technician Joseph Sekiku, in Karagwe, who created a telecentre on his property with the help of small grants. The Sengerema telecentre, some 200 km away, is the result of several donor and community initiatives engaging a range of stakeholders.
The study stresses the need for expanding full internet access, using wireless networks, arguing against the common view that more rapidly expanding mobile phone networks may be able to fill the need for connectivity. There are different possible models for sustainability, he argues, but all require long-term investment in strengthening local technical capacity. Far more important than formal training is identifying potential local "techies" and fostering their skills for troubleshooting and innovation.

Original_Search_Title: Lagos - Haven of Opportunities, Hell of Miseries
Headline: Nigeria: Lagos - Haven of Opportunities, Hell of Miseries
Body_Text:
The poor arrive in Lagos to escape the poverty of rural Nigeria and nearby countries; the rich come to the city to escape the violence in other parts of the country. About 6,000 people move to Lagos everyday, according to estimates, adding to the already bursting population of about 15 million.
The United Nations (UN) estimates that the population of Lagos will swell to 25 million by 2015. What are the special attractions in Lagos? Can't these migrants look elsewhere for survival? group business editor, Rotimi Durojaiye, writes on the good and bad side of Lagos, the commercial hub of sub-Saharan Africa.

Original_Search_Title: Forget EPAs With Europe, Go for GSP-Plus, Activists Say
Headline: Africa: Forget EPAs With Europe, Go for GSP-Plus, Activists Say
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Developing countries negotiating with the European Union for an economic partnership agreement (EPA) or a free trade agreement should apply for "GSP-Plus" status with the EU, Kenya Civil Society activists say.
The Generalised System of Preferences (GSP-Plus) status provides non-reciprocal preferences to a large part of the exports of developing country compared with the non-reciprocal nature of the EPAs.

Original_Search_Title: Bottom Falls Out of Vanilla Boom
Headline: Uganda: Bottom Falls Out of Vanilla Boom
Body_Text:
Nairobi — After years of showing promise and being touted as Uganda's best export hope following a boom in the 1990s that almost saw it overtake coffee in exports, vanilla is now branded a risky venture.
At the height of the boon in 2005, vanilla went for Ush100,000 ($57) a kilogramme but this has now fallen to as low as Ush2,000 ($1.14) a kilogramme.

Original_Search_Title: Danish Queen Starts Visit
Headline: Tanzania: Danish Queen Starts Visit
Body_Text:
Her Majesty Queen Margrethe II of Denmark and her husband, His Royal Highness The Prince Consort, start their official four-day visit to Tanzania today.
The monarch and her delegation arrived in Dar es Salaam on Saturday night. President Jakaya Kikwete will host bthe royal couple at an official function at Karimjee Hall today.

Original_Search_Title: Who is After Speaker Bankole?
Headline: Nigeria: Who is After Speaker Bankole?
Body_Text:
Even before the controversy over the N2.3 billion 380 units of Peugeot 407 purchase saga broke out, there has always been this fear that somebody was after Speaker Dimeji Bankole.
Shortly after his emergence as Speaker, there was an allegation that the Speaker had not performed the mandatory National Youth Service Scheme. It took several weeks of counter offensive by the Bankole team to douse the tension and prove his innocence.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Salutes Local Processors
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Salutes Local Processors
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has saluted young entrepreneurs engaged in value-addition to Ugandan products for both local consumption and export.
This, he said, was a wake-up effort to banish export of raw materials.

Original_Search_Title: Obasanjo Ceded Bakassi to Win Nobel Peace Prize -Orji Kalu
Headline: Cameroon: Obasanjo Ceded Bakassi to Win Nobel Peace Prize -Orji Kalu
Body_Text:
Lagos — Former Abia State Governor and the Chairman of Board of Trustees, Progressive People's Alliance (PPA), Orji Kalu, has disclosed that former President Olusegun Obasanjo ceded the oil-rich Bakassi Peninsula to Cameroon to further his plan to win the Nobel Peace prize.
The former governor also alleged that the cause of the rift between him and Obasanjo was his inordinate quest to become a life president.

Original_Search_Title: With Tense Calm in Congo, Time to Assess Damage
Headline: Nigeria: With Tense Calm in Congo, Time to Assess Damage
Body_Text:
Lagos — Famished villagers were huddled in ramshackle houses, surviving off raw carrots and hard, unripe papayas.
Victorious rebel soldiers, shirtless and chiseled, exercised on a former United Nations military base, lifting a makeshift dumbbell fashioned from plundered United Nations equipment.

Original_Search_Title: Only a New Constitution Will Save Us
Headline: Kenya: Only a New Constitution Will Save Us
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Even before Justice Philip Waki's damning report on the post-election violence took centre stage, it was clear from pronouncements, and the lack thereof, that the issue of constitutional review had been put on the back burner.
Most have blocked from their minds the memories of the violence and, as they focus on 2012, the danger of its recurrence seems lost on them or they simply do not care.

Original_Search_Title: We are Doomed If We Don't Respect Our Rules
Headline: Kenya: We are Doomed If We Don't Respect Our Rules
Body_Text:
Nairobi — After spending decades observing myself and my fellow human beings, I am forced to come to a somewhat cynical conclusion: most of us only behave well when we are compelled to do so.
When left to his own devices and untrammeled by the demands of morality and legality, the average person will do all the wrong things.

Original_Search_Title: The Buck Stops With Kibaki and Raila
Headline: Kenya: The Buck Stops With Kibaki and Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — This is a letter to both President Kibaki and Prime Minister Raila Odinga. I write to you for two reasons.
One, Kenyans are angry over the Waki Report. They are baying for blood; the blood of some big fish. Their argument? When you arrested their children in the January madness, you refused to give them amnesty. But when your buddies in Cabinet were put on some "List" you started talking amnesty!

Original_Search_Title: Seeing the Back of Bra Alpha
Headline: Namibia: Seeing the Back of Bra Alpha
Body_Text:
So, the race for the presidency of Athletics Namibia has been set between the continent's most celebrated athlete and the country's most unpopular sports administrator.
Many of us in the business of sport are well aware that knives have been drawn and some affiliates are anxiously waiting to see the back of Alpha aka "Barry White" Kangueehi following his turbulent tenure at the helm of the troubled organization.

Original_Search_Title: Bankole and the 380 Cars - Matters Arising
Headline: Nigeria: Bankole and the 380 Cars - Matters Arising
Body_Text:
Eseme Sunday Eyiboh, Chairman of the House of Representatives Committee on Media and Public Affairs, is naturally humorous. Whenever he speaks with journalists, grey-haired Eyiboh would crack jokes that even the legendary Nigerian commedian, Ali Baba would become envious of.
But on Monday of the penultimate week, he almost lost his cool at a news conference at the National Assembly complex, no thanks to the contents of a letter written by Lagos Lawyer, Festus Keyamo, to Speaker Dimeji Bankole, asking him to explain the alleged fraud in the purchase of vehicles meant for the standing committees to conduct their oversight functions. The development was a baptism of sort for Eyiboh who was barely 11 days in office as the House spokesman. All the same, he was equal to the task, confidently defending the Speaker and the House leadership.

Original_Search_Title: Reap From Honey - Museveni
Headline: Africa: Reap From Honey - Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has advised African countries to take advantage of the $3b world honey market trade.
Addressing participants at the launch of Honey Trade Africa (ApiTrade) at a conference at Hotel Africana in Kampala last week, the President said regional honey trade was in line with regional integration. "We need to come together and support these regional groups to flourish. Africa produces rich honey for export but this goes unrecognised all over the world. ApiTrade will recognise our honey," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Four Questions for the Cabinet Team
Headline: Kenya: Four Questions for the Cabinet Team
Body_Text:
Nairobi — This is a letter to the Cabinet. Greetings and good health to the 43 of you.
I will go straight to the point: there are warmongers amongst you. Some are on the Waki List, others are not. But I am not worried by those on the Waki List.

Original_Search_Title: The Cutting Edge
Headline: Kenya: The Cutting Edge
Body_Text:
The Waki report on the post-election violence, in which some ministers and MPs have been implicated in sponsoring hooligans to kill fellow Kenyans, is both good and bad in equal measure, says Alfred Muhindi.
"It's a double-edged sword whose recommendations are bound to have far-reaching implications. But the irony is that we shall regret whether we fully implement the report or not. We've no alternative but to choose the best form of regret!"

Original_Search_Title: Aronda to Evict 100 Families in Kiboga
Headline: Uganda: Aronda to Evict 100 Families in Kiboga
Body_Text:
Kampala — Over 100 families of struggling peasants in Kiboga District have been ordered to immediately vacate a 640-acre private Mailo land, which Gen. Aronda Nyakairima, the chief of Defence Forces, wants to urgently acquire for private investment.
Gen. Aronda confirmed to Daily Monitor that he made advance payments to Mr Gideon Kibirango, who claims to be the registered owner of the 259-hectare land in Singo, but the transaction could turn sour due to ownership wrangles.

Original_Search_Title: Obama to Borrow Museveni's Lubengo
Headline: Uganda: Obama to Borrow Museveni's Lubengo
Body_Text:
With barely two weeks to go to the presidential election in the United States, the front-runner, Senator Barack Steve Hussein Obama, is leaving nothing to chance. The senator, who has virtually wrapped up the election, is writing to President Yoweri Museveni asking to borrow "some stuff" that will enable him jump over the only remaining hurdle in his race to the White House.
American voters agree that Obama has got pretty much everything - intelligence, looks, charisma - that any candidate could hope for. His only pitfall, according to the pundits, is that he is "too serious" to the point of appearing to be aloof from the common folks. He has been accused - and this is intended to be a criticism - of being "professorial".

Original_Search_Title: Draw Good for Country - Abedi
Headline: Ghana: Draw Good for Country - Abedi
Body_Text:
Ghana legend Abedi Ayew Pele claims Ghana has been handed a good draw in their quest to reach the 2010 World Cup in South Africa.
The draw on Wednesday pitted the Black Stars against West Africa neighbours Benin, Mali and Sudan with the process due to begin in March for the Black Stars with a home game against Benin.

Original_Search_Title: Third Time Lucky to Dream Island in the Sun!
Headline: Zimbabwe: Third Time Lucky to Dream Island in the Sun!
Body_Text:
I"LL be writing about the most wonderful, colourful, luxurious, welcoming, relaxing resort enjoyed in 50-odd years of travelling for profit or pleasure, from (alphabetically) Abu Dhabi to Zululand, here and in Zimbabwe Independent for a few issues.
And it was third time lucky for a trip to Indigo Bay Island Resort and Spa, an ultra-swish destination for the rich, famous and wannabe r&fs on Bazaruto Island in the Indian Ocean, off Vilankulo, Mozambique.

Original_Search_Title: The Cutting Edge
Headline: Kenya: The Cutting Edge
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Traffic police manning busy roundabouts in Nairobi do a hopeless job because they don't get a clear view of the jam in the various directions, says Kamichore Mutindira, adding that he now has a solution.
"At the big roundabouts at the Nyayo National Stadium, GPO, and University Way, platforms should be built so that the officers controlling traffic can stand on top and have a clear view of the jam. This works well in Kinshasa, Congo."

Original_Search_Title: Global Financial Crisis - Cameroon Feels the Pinch
Headline: Cameroon: Global Financial Crisis - Cameroon Feels the Pinch
Body_Text:
The forestry sector becomes first victim, other commodities to follow if situation persists.
It sounded so far when news of the credit award system in the United States igniting a paralysis on the country's banking institutions reached Cameroon. Eminent economists and some authoritative government personalities dispelled all fears the effect will hit the country. A few weeks after, the hand writing is becoming clear on the wall. It is no longer an American or European affair. The Global Financial crisis has come to confirm the interconnectivity of nations which has rendered the world a global village.

Original_Search_Title: Danish Queen to Visit
Headline: Tanzania: Danish Queen to Visit
Body_Text:
Her Majesty Queen Margret the II of Demmark and his Royal Highness Prince Consort will visit Tanzania on November 3 at the invitation of President Jakaya Kikwete.
The focus of the historic four-day state visit will be on development cooperation, investment opportunities and culture.

Original_Search_Title: NRM Critics Need Mental Check-Up, Says Museveni
Headline: Uganda: NRM Critics Need Mental Check-Up, Says Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — PEOPLE who think that the Ugandan economy has not developed under the National Resistance Movement (NRM) government should go for mental check-up, President Yoweri Museveni has said.
Museveni, who was on Saturday evening addressing a public rally at Rukungiri district stadium, said: "I have heard people telling lies that some parts of Uganda are neglected. They need to go to hospital for mental check-up. Even a blind person can see the development in this country."

Original_Search_Title: Waki's Verdict On Kibaki And Raila
Headline: Kenya: Waki's Verdict On Kibaki And Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki and Prime Minister Raila Odinga are not blameless over the post-election violence in which more than 1,200 people were killed early this year.
Even though the two principals, alongside former UN secretary general Kofi Annan were last Friday honoured by the University of Nairobi for restoring peace in the country, the Waki Commission has referred to them over a number of acts of omission and commission that may have led to the violence that threatened to tear up Kenya at the beginning of this year.

Original_Search_Title: Mending Broken Political Fences
Headline: Uganda: Mending Broken Political Fences
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni's week-long Prosperity for All (Bonna Bagaggawale) campaign working tour in Rukungiri District that was concluded today turned into a big political fete.
Residents rushed into the streets in numbers chanting NRM slogans. This brought back to memory the huge turnout at his rallies in the 2001 and 2006 elections.

Original_Search_Title: Horticulture Exports Fall By 61.6 Percent
Headline: Rwanda: Horticulture Exports Fall By 61.6 Percent
Body_Text:
Kigali — Rwanda's horticulture exports have dropped from $341.658 (Frw188.4m) in August last year to $131.171 (Frw72.3m) in August this year, according to Rwanda Investment and Export Promotion Agency (Riepa).
Rwanda Horticulture Development Authority (RHODA) attributes the fall in export revenue to the low value of horticulture exports which does not attract much demand on the international market.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Tours Rukungiri as Besigye Wins Grassroot Poll
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Tours Rukungiri as Besigye Wins Grassroot Poll
Body_Text:
President Yoweri Museveni has called on Ugandans to work hard and eradicate poverty in their homes.
This, he said, should be done by embracing the government development programmes like Prosperity for All.

Original_Search_Title: The loss of an Illustrious Son
Headline: Uganda: In Sebunya Uganda Has Lost One of Its Most Illustrious Sons
Body_Text:
Tonight when the sun sets, Dr Israel Kibirige Sebunya will have arrived at another secure location. But I think he will arrive there well rested and comfortable.
The country's last bedside visit was conducted by Owek. John B. Walusimbi, Katikkiro of Buganda. Normally defiant to a fault, Kibirige was doing his final laps. Those private rooms in Mulago Hospital give death the dubious privilege of arriving and departing in secret.

Original_Search_Title: Red Faces Over EPAs Red Herring
Headline: Africa: Red Faces Over EPAs Red Herring
Body_Text:
Brussels — Senior European Union officials have admitted being embarrassed at how the bloc's own procedures are delaying them from signing economic partnership agreements (EPAs) with Africa.
During 2007 the European Commission repeatedly insisted that it was necessary to conclude trade accords with African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) countries by the end of that year.

Original_Search_Title: Siavonga Tourism Now On Website
Headline: Zambia: Siavonga Tourism Now On Website
Body_Text:
THE feeling among the business community in Siavonga is that they have not had a fair share of tourism promotion over the years.
Livingstone, home to the Mosi-oa-Tunya, according to them, has always had an edge over them. While they agree that there might not be a lot particularly wrong with that approach since Livingstone is the tourist capital of the country, they believe they should also be receiving a reasonably fair share in promotions as a tourist destination.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Traders to Focus on Regional Markets
Headline: Uganda: Banana Traders to Focus on Regional Markets
Body_Text:
Kampala — Experts have called on banana traders to take advantage of local and regional markets as European countries open their markets to imports from Latin America.
The experts at the just concluded Africa International Banana Conference held in Mombasa, Kenya, last week, said this move will boost production and incomes of the poor.

Original_Search_Title: Besigye Lying About Rwanda - Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Besigye Lying About Rwanda - Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has attacked opposition leader Kizza Besigye for saying neighbouring Rwanda pays its civil servants higher salaries than Uganda.
"It is true that the Rwandan Government pays good salaries to its workers. But Besigye should also have said that there is no Universal Primary Education (UPE) and Universal Secondary Education (USE) in Rwanda," the President said while addressing a public rally at Kebisoni county headquarters in Rukungiri yesterday.

Original_Search_Title: Belgian Mission Explores Business Opportunities
Headline: Rwanda: Belgian Mission Explores Business Opportunities
Body_Text:
For the sixth time, a Belgian business mission has visited the country to assess the investment opportunities as well as looking for partnerships between both business communities. So far, the missions have yielded little result.
The main aim of the mission was to encourage Belgian companies not only to review business opportunities but to also to invest and to establish themselves in the country or to form business partnerships with their Rwandan counterparts.

Original_Search_Title: The End of the World?
Headline: Rwanda: The End of the World?
Body_Text:
The heavy rains and winds that hit some parts of the country recently, have caused a lost of damage but made no victims. Yet in Kirehe, in Eastern Province, some residents thought the end of the world had come.
It was just after lunch, at around 2 pm. Yvonne Uwamariya was bathing the kids to prepare them for their afternoon nap. Suddenly, the weather changed.

Original_Search_Title: Japanese Varsity Introduces Banana Textile
Headline: Rwanda: Japanese Varsity Introduces Banana Textile
Body_Text:
Kigali — A 10-member delegation from Japan arrives in Rwanda today to conduct a seminar and workshop on the technology to convert banana fibre into textile.
The team, composed of professors and students from Tama Art University and officers from UNIDO-Japan will on Friday showcase the technology to a high-profile audience during a seminar at KIST.

Original_Search_Title: How 'Bananas' Give Country Shelter From the Storm
Headline: South Africa: How 'Bananas' Give Country Shelter From the Storm
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — IN 2002, SA lost 22 banks. Some went under, others were bought out. Many chose to relinquish their licences because the market had lost confidence in smaller banks and it simply wasn't worth having a licence any more.
But they weren't all small: the banks that went down included the seventh-largest , Saambou, and the contagion caused the demise of the sixth-largest, BOE, which was bailed out by the government and then bought by Nedbank.

Original_Search_Title: House Selection Committee Yet to Decide - Bankole
Headline: Nigeria: House Selection Committee Yet to Decide - Bankole
Body_Text:
Abuja — Speaker, House of Representatives. Honourable Dimeji Bankole, at the first sitting after the Salah break, said the selection committee charged with the responsibility of re-constituting the House Committees was yet to conclude its assignment.
Members who returned to the chambers yesterday, expecting release of list of members of the 72 Standing Committees were disappointed, as Bankole who incidentally chairs the Committee said though the Committees have been re-constituted, it would meet later in the afternoon to finalise its position.

Original_Search_Title: Police Block Kabaka Tour
Headline: Uganda: Police Block Kabaka Tour of Nakasongola
Body_Text:
Nakasongola — The government has barred the Kabaka of Buganda, Ronald Muwenda Mutebi, from visiting Nakasongola District headquarters where he was scheduled to celebrate the kingdom's 46th independence anniversary today.
Buganda attained Independence as a federal state from the British colonial government on October 8, 1962 but the Kabaka's visit to Buluuli County headquarters, which is also the Nakasongola District headquarters, had been strongly opposed by the Baruuli, who are seeking a breakaway from the kingdom.

Original_Search_Title: Kampala City on High Security Alert
Headline: Uganda: Kampala City on High Security Alert
Body_Text:
Kampala — SECURITY has been tightened in public places in Kampala in response to the terror alert issued by the Police on Thursday.
Security checks and screening have intensified at hotels, taxi parks, bus stations, shopping malls, markets, government installations and foreign embassies.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Drags DCE to NPP Disciplinary Committee
Headline: Ghana: Minister Drags DCE to NPP Disciplinary Committee
Body_Text:
The bickering and mudslinging among the New Patriotic Party faithful in Dangbe West seem to be intensifying, with the Deputy Minister of Women& Children's Affairs and the parliamentary candidate of Shai Osudoku, Mr. Dugan dragging the District Chief Executive(DCE) of the area to the NPP Disciplinary Committee (DC).
Mr. Dugan is seeking punitive action from both the DC and the Chief of Staff against the DCE, Hon. Michael Adjowerh-Nortey, for misconduct.

Original_Search_Title: Much Ado About Cabinet Reshuffle
Headline: Nigeria: Much Ado About Cabinet Reshuffle
Body_Text:
One of the definitions of insanity is doing the same thing over and over and expecting different results. Unlike most nations on planet earth, in Nigeria, the more things change, the more they stay the same.
We have tried different systems of government from military dictatorship to parliamentary system to unitary system to presidential system of government and the federal structure of government that we pretend to practise. We often think that the bane of our problems as a nation is the system of government that we adopt rather than the substance of the government and the role those actors in the system we adopt at any particular point in time play.

Original_Search_Title: Girl Child Education - Kwali LG to Adopt Community Approach
Headline: Nigeria: Girl Child Education - Kwali LG to Adopt Community Approach
Body_Text:
In a bid to encourage girlchild education, the Kwali Area Council of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), is to adopt a community approach through sensitizing the traditional rulers and village heads.
The Acting Chairman of the council, Mr. Danlami Saleh Yebu, who stated this while speaking to newsmen yesterday, said the council will liaise with the elected councillors in the ten wards of the council to educate parents on the significance of girl child education.

Original_Search_Title: Surviving in Kisenyi Ghetto
Headline: Uganda: Surviving in Kisenyi Ghetto
Body_Text:
Yusuf Ali, a 27-year-old resident in the ghetto of Kisenyi has just been released from Kitalya Prison today without bail, trial, or any known legal procedure. "I used to shout at the policemen everyday, threatening them that I would revenge.
I think this scared them and they decided to release me," he said. He has been in jail for the last 17 days, since an SPC policeman carried him away. "He found me here (pointing to a seat at the corner of a house) with my friends and said I was a criminal, and forced me to leave with him," he narrates.

Original_Search_Title: The Cutting Edge
Headline: Kenya: The Cutting Edge
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kenya is at peace with its neighbours, so why do we need the 33 deadly T-72 tanks in the cargo ship seized by Somalia pirates? asks Mohinder Dhillon.
He says the country has too many problems, including the crisis of the people displaced by post-election violence, an ailing public health sector, and a poorly funded education system, to splash out cash on such military hardware.

Original_Search_Title: This Woman is Optimistic About Country's Future
Headline: Nigeria: This Woman is Optimistic About Country's Future
Body_Text:
Ibadan — It sounds incredible that Nigeria will rank among the best global economy in no distant future. But Chief (Mrs) Bola Doherty minced no words in arriving at just that conclusion.
She is of the conviction that we will soon get there "if all our endowed human and material resources as well as the potentialities are put into full use to this noble cause."

Original_Search_Title: Sameg is Best Foreign Exhibitor at Rwanda Fair
Headline: Uganda: Sameg is Best Foreign Exhibitor at Rwanda Fair
Body_Text:
Kampala — A UGANDAN firm, Sameg Chemical Products, has won this year's Foreign Exhibitors Award at the Rwanda International Trade Fair.
The award was won at the 11th Rwanda trade fair that ended last month.

Original_Search_Title: What the Heck - Let Egypt Too Have a Piece of the Pie
Headline: Uganda: What the Heck - Let Egypt Too Have a Piece of the Pie
Body_Text:
It always begins as a rumour that is strenuously denied. This is followed by vague and ambiguous half- hearted confirmations.
Before you know it, there is heavy construction equipment on the site as yet another piece of prime real estate is gobbled up by foreigners in the name of foreign investment, often in total disregard of environment protection laws.

Original_Search_Title: Country Should Learn From Cuba
Headline: Zimbabwe: Country Should Learn From Cuba
Body_Text:
Harare — EVERY year for well over a decade now without fail, Cuba presents to the United Nations a report outlining the effects of sanctions on the populace of the largest island in the Caribbean.
In that report, Cuba outlines the impact of the embargo that the United States imposed on that country just because the people there opted to go with a political, economic, social and indeed cultural ideology that is different from that espoused by Washington.

Original_Search_Title: Democracy in a Jungle of Guns?
Headline: Nigeria: Democracy in a Jungle of Guns?
Body_Text:
The above caption was attributed to a son of a rich Palestinian merchant, an Egyptian trained engineer, and leader of Fattah, later Palestinian Liberation Organisation (PLO). It is no other person than the charismatic, indifagable, articulate, and at times, controversial Yasser Arafat of blessed memory. Obviously, the headgear trademark anti-Zionist man with the olive branch and freedom fighter was referring to the prevailing situation in the Gaza Strip, West Bank, Golan height, and Ramala chaotically rumbustious territories.
Africa had been in and out of the jungles of guns world, way before our unholy encounter with the Europeans. Preceding the arrival of our uninvited double-minded guests, empires and kingdoms wrestled for land and territorial expansions. Some opine that at that time, there were no guns, talk less of having a jungle of them. Yes, that is quite true. Are we then at liberty to say jungle of bows, arrows, daggers, and knives?

Original_Search_Title: State of Suspended Development After 50 Years of Independence
Headline: Guinea: State of Suspended Development After 50 Years of Independence
Body_Text:
Conakry — As regional leaders gathered in Conakry on 2 October to celebrate Guinea's 50 years of independence from France, on the other side of the capital, doctors emerging from a ten-day strike said when it comes to healthcare, there is little to celebrate.
"There has not been much progress in the health sector in Guinea over the past few decades. Health centres and hospitals still do not have enough equipment or staff. And we experience frequent difficulty in acquiring even basic life-saving medicines," said government health adviser Dr. Sékou Doukouré.

Original_Search_Title: 'No Policy Change' and Other Myths of the Moment
Headline: South Africa: 'No Policy Change' and Other Myths of the Moment
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — THE tumultuous political developments of the past week, which ushered in Kgalema Motlanthe as democratic SA's third president, caught many analysts and much of the chattering classes off guard, despite the obvious signs that change in the African National Congress (ANC) was afoot.
It appears as if myth-making is becoming a national pastime when it comes to analysing our political dynamics , especially when it concerns the ruling party. One myth, soon to be debunked, is that there will be no policy changes after the seismic shift in the ANC and government in the aftermath of Polokwane and last week's political drama.

Original_Search_Title: Clans Protest Election of Busoga King
Headline: Uganda: Clans Protest Election of Busoga King
Body_Text:
Iganga — A rift has erupted over the election of a new Kyabazinga of Busoga due to take place on Friday.
A group of 80 clan leaders signed a declaration on Saturday saying there was no need to elect a new king to succeed the late Henry Wako Muloki.

Original_Search_Title: Number 10 - Jay Jay Okocha's Delicious Number
Headline: Nigeria: Number 10 - Jay Jay Okocha's Delicious Number
Body_Text:
Lagos — Ten is the magic number. Number 10 is what pleasant memories are made of. 10 was the number made famous by the irrepressible and inimitable Jay Jay Okocha, the Nigerian darling of international football. Charming, most endearing, very entertaining and delightful, Jay Jay has built an institution that tries to capture the Jay Jay spirit. His joie de vivre, airy disposition and colourful life style inspired a lovely restaurant, sports bar and weekend nite club on Victoria Island's Saka Tinubu Street, in Lagos.
What better name to call a Jay Jay creation than Number 10 and its popularity since it was opened in December 2007, has made it as famous as the other Number 10, the Downing Street residence of the British Prime Minister. Toss in the metal and weapons detectors at the entrance to the building and you can appreciate the comparison, for both play host to VIP's and thus have to ensure their safety in a crazy world.

Original_Search_Title: Anxiety As Mark Moves
Headline: Nigeria: Anxiety As Mark Moves
Body_Text:
Sufuyan Ojeifo writes on the politicking in the Senate over the planned reshuffle of standing committee membership by the Senate leadership under Senator David Mark
Last week, the Senate Leader, Senator Teslim Folarin hinted, for the umpteenth time, that the leadership of the Senate would reconstitute the standing committees of the upper chamber. He first dropped the hint a day or two after the Court of Appeal sitting in Jos validated the election of the Senate President, Senator David Mark as senator representing Benue South Senatorial District

Original_Search_Title: Stop Glorifying the Gun Culture
Headline: Uganda: Stop Glorifying the Gun Culture
Body_Text:
Time is now for all Ugandans of sound mind and goodwill to stop glorifying the gun culture in our country. The police leadership appears to be obsessed with militarism and a willy-nilly attitude towards solving all crime related issues no matter how petty they may be.
The media has been awash with the news that the police intends to arm its traffic section to counter errant motorists and to emulate other countries like Indonesia, Thailand and the USA. Comparing Uganda with the United States or Indonesia is missing the point by far. We are a banana republic, whose citizens cherish corruption, believe in tribal and political patronage, know nothing about human values and hate accountability like poison.

Original_Search_Title: Firm Introduces Flavored Anti-Malaria Drugs
Headline: Nigeria: Firm Introduces Flavored Anti-Malaria Drugs
Body_Text:
Lagos — The aggressive drive by Tyonex Nigeria Ltd, one of Nigeria 's leading pharmaceutical firms, to rid the nation of the scourge of malaria has received a quantum boost with the introduction of its range of anti-malaria products.
Leading the attack is Actimax, a powerful combination of Artemether and Lumefantrine, for maximum action against malaria in both children and adults. The suspension is a child-sensitive presentation in three tasty flavours - banana, orange and raspberry. It is said to be the first triple flavoured anti-malaria suspension to be manufactured in Nigeria . Both the suspension and tablets are produced by Divine Essential Formulations, the manufacturing arm of Tyonex Nigeria Ltd.

Original_Search_Title: Trying to Read Country After Nicholson
Headline: South Africa: Trying to Read Country After Nicholson
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — IN 1970 Lord Patrick Devlin wrote in The Judge: "It is a great temptation to cast the judiciary as an elite which will bypass the traffic-laden ways of the democratic process."
He continued so: "But it would only apparently be a bypass. In truth it would be a road that would never rejoin the highway but would lead inevitably, however long and winding the path, to the authoritarian state."

Original_Search_Title: Thugs Rape, Kill Pump Attendant
Headline: Uganda: Thugs Rape, Kill Pump Attendant
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Police in Masaka are hunting for thugs who raped and killed a pump attendant and shot another at a filling station in Kinoni, Masaka district.
The southern regional Police commander, Andrew Sorowen, identified the deceased as Immaculate Nakachwa and the injured as Richard Lubega.

Original_Search_Title: Money to Educate Artists
Headline: Namibia: Money to Educate Artists
Body_Text:
Windhoek — FNB took another bold step to reach out to emerging and established Namibian artists and musicians in the music industry, sponsoring N$21 500 to the Oruuano of Namibian Artists' Union in Windhoek yesterday.
The sponsorship will help the Oruuano of Namibian Artists' Union to hold a one-day workshop for Namibian artists, to take place on October 01 at the GZ Functions Hall at Dr W Kulz and Bismarck streets.

Original_Search_Title: NDC Hints On Manifesto
Headline: Ghana: NDC Hints On Manifesto
Body_Text:
The National Democratic Congress (NDC) yesterday made public the highlights of their manifesto themed - "Building a Better Ghana", which is to be launched in two weeks.
The highlights, according to the Director of Communications of NDC, Ms Hannah S. Tetteh, were made public via the internet, due to requests for information on the manifesto.

Original_Search_Title: Motlanthe to Succeed Mbeki
Headline: South Africa: Motlanthe to Succeed Mbeki
Body_Text:
South Africa's ruling African National Congress (ANC) yesterday chose a caretaker president to replace Mr. Thabo Mbeki, who obeyed a party order and resigned on Sunday evening.
Sources within the party have named deputy leader Kgalema Motlanthe, an ally of party leader Jacob Zuma, who made clear his backing for Motlanthe as he pledged that the party would ensure a smooth transition and economic policy continuity despite the biggest political crisis since the end of apartheid in 1994. "We have in cabinet many experienced ministers, including the deputy president of the ANC, Kgalema Motlanthe. I'm convinced that if given that responsibility, he would be equal to the task," said Zuma, in his first public remarks since Mbeki announced he would resign in the face of ANC demands to quit.

Original_Search_Title: Women Wield Fair Trade Tools to Beat Poverty
Headline: Uganda: Women Wield Fair Trade Tools to Beat Poverty
Body_Text:
Kampala — Producing baskets and mats in central Uganda has traditionally been women's work. Women made these items for use in homes. The National Association of Women Organisations in Uganda (NAWOU) has changed this practice into a powerful force fighting poverty.
The organisation has a big crafts collection centre in the east African country's capital of Kampala. Baskets, toys, mats and hand-made cotton cloth can be found there, among other things. The collection centre operates according to fair trade principles and is affiliated to the International Fair Trade Association (IFAT).

Original_Search_Title: Statement Issued by Archbishop Emeritus Desmond Tutu - Deeply Disturbed
Headline: South Africa: Statement Issued by Archbishop Emeritus Desmond Tutu - Deeply Disturbed
Body_Text:
Milnerton — I am deeply disturbed by recent events in our beloved county. The so-called recalling of the President of our land fits the pattern of the settling of scores and the throwing about of weight that has happened post-Polokwane.
Why sack two Premiers with only a few months of their terms left to run and then to re-employ one of them in the Presidency if it is not to prove that there are new cocks of the walk? Why humiliate the nation’s President in this fashion like the two Premiers with only a few months of his term of office left and that without giving him the chance to respond to any charges that the NEC might have laid?

Original_Search_Title: Tutu Warns of 'Banana Republic' Future
Headline: South Africa: Tutu Warns of 'Banana Republic' Future
Body_Text:
Cape Town — Archbishop Desmond Tutu said yesterday he was "deeply disturbed" by President Thabo Mbeki's "humiliating" axing, which meant SA had been subordinated to a political party.
In a statement in defence of Mbeki, he warned SA could be on its way to becoming a banana republic.

Original_Search_Title: Fascism Collides With Rule of Law
Headline: Nigeria: Fascism Collides With Rule of Law
Body_Text:
I go shout O!
I go shout plenti O!

Original_Search_Title: The New Farmer
Headline: East Africa: The New Farmer
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Inflation is at 27 per cent and the prices of fuel and food are rising every day. The only thing that is not going up is that all-important figure on the payslip. How to cope?
That is the dilemma facing many of Kenya's urban dwellers, most of whom work in low-paying casual jobs. Some are turning to an age-old mechanism -- growing their own food -- to ride out the inflationary wave.

Original_Search_Title: MDC-T - Keep Your Demos in Check
Headline: Zimbabwe: MDC-T - Keep Your Demos in Check
Body_Text:
Harare — AS before, the media has once again failed in their duty to inform and enlighten through well-founded interpretation of events of the week. Copies of the agreement underpinning the hopes of an inclusive government in Zimbabwe were made available to all media houses. The media, thus, have no excuse for rigging facts and dishing out harebrained interpretations.
Well, before one even gets into the substance of the agreement, one is hard put to establish where the notion of Government of National Unity came from. It is not there in the Dar Declaration. It is not there in Sadc communiqués.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Tours Are No Poverty Pill
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Tours Are No Poverty Pill
Body_Text:
President Museveni is continuing with his countrywide tour to find out whether his, rather NRM's, main campaign slogan - Bonna Bagagawale (Prosperity for All) is working. After Busoga, Bugisu and Teso, the President is in Kigezi giving tips on how people can overcome poverty, a good thing for a leader to do.
The presidential tours have received less coverage than they deserve. This is because the media is obsessed with the Shs 11.2 billion new Kampala land dealers Amama Mbabazi and Amos Nzeyi have bagged by selling 463 acres of land in Temangalo to NSSF. I am not in anyway suggesting that this is a small story.

Original_Search_Title: Mbarara Tops in Murder - Police
Headline: Uganda: Mbarara Tops in Murder - Police
Body_Text:
Kampala — A total of 243 murders have been reported in south-western Uganda with Mbarara and Bushenyi districts topping the list, the Police have said.
Addressing the press recently, regional Police chief Alfarouk Muyirima said from January 1 to July 31, the Police recorded 52 murder cases in Bushenyi and 51 in Mbarara.

Original_Search_Title: A Total of 24,000 Tons to be Exported to Portugal
Headline: Ethiopia: A Total of 24,000 Tons to be Exported to Portugal
Body_Text:
Ethiopia will export 24,000tn of raw sugar in the current fiscal year to European markets duty-free under the Everything But Arms (EBA) initiative, a special arrangement EU has granted to least developed countries. The Sugar Development Agency (SDA) received a letter from EU, via the Ethiopian Embassy in Brussels, stating that Ethiopia is allowed to export the stated amount, which is up by 3,300tn from last year.
The sugar destined for the EU - to be obtained from Wonji-Shoa Sugar Factory, one of the three sugar factories in Ethiopia - will be exported to Portugal as of January this year.

Original_Search_Title: New Road Under Construction in Alebu
Headline: Eritrea: New Road Under Construction in Alebu
Body_Text:
Haikota — A 5-Km.-long road is under construction in Alebu, Haikota sub-zone, which links the newly constructed Gash Bridge with the Haikota-Tessenei route. The road being constructed through ASBECO Construction Company is expected to be finalized within three months.
The supervisor of the project, Eng. Mehari Girmai, indicated that the road would play a significant role in transporting bananas and tomatoes produced around Fanko and Gerset to the newly set up Banatom Factory in Alebu. Besides, it would make due contribution in facilitating development activities in the sub-zones of Haikota, Tessenei, Golij and Laelai-Gash, he added.

Original_Search_Title: Presidential Faltering Steps
Headline: Nigeria: Presidential Faltering Steps
Body_Text:
Any assessment of the one year existence of the Yar'adua administration's policy shift reveals the attitude of a regime startled from the soporific effect of a benign anaesthesia administered for an overdue major operation.
Sometimes, they seem right, and at other times, they seem so wrong. The general opinion is that most time it is in a supine position. The controversy over Mr President's health notwithstanding there is no gainsaying the fact that he has sprung a few surprises. The sacking of the military chiefs on the eve of his departure for a major controversial trip is an ace scored on military adventurism. If he had asked any military consultant, they would have told him not to even think of it. Take a closer look at that decision and it shows you a man who knows his onions and appears to have cemented his foot firmly in the floor to take serious risks.

Original_Search_Title: Learning From the Obama Campaign
Headline: Kenya: Learning From the Obama Campaign
Body_Text:
Twenty years ago, almost to the day, I left Kenya for the USA having been awarded a scholarship for a Master of Laws at Harvard Law School. There I was, a typical rural youth, from the slopes as Whispers would put it, arriving in America. The normal affair, then, of many many prayers, matatus thronged in banana leaves and the entire clan coming to see me off is a memory I shall never forget.
Nor is the three-piece pin-stripped suit befitting a young lawyer, courtesy of boom, going to fade from my mind. Especially because I was bathed in serious sweat when I landed in Boston, shocked that America could actually feel as hot as Mombasa or Kisumu (this was before I had ever travelled to Northern Kenya….). No one had told me about the summer heat and humidity in late August.

Original_Search_Title: Ramadan - Traders Make Brisk Business
Headline: Nigeria: Ramadan - Traders Make Brisk Business
Body_Text:
Lagos — As Ramadan, the Muslim fasting month, got underway, Muslims have besieged major markets to shop for foodstuffs while traders make brisk business.
Some Lagosians who spoke to Daily Trust said they had observed that during the fasting period, prices of fruits and other commodities usually skyrocket, thus they had to shop now to avoid paying more for the commodities during the fasting period.

Original_Search_Title: Political Activists Want Interim National Govt
Headline: Nigeria: Political Activists Want Interim National Govt
Body_Text:
Lagos — Nigerians United for Democracy (NUD), a platform comprising political parties, civil society and self-determination groups in the country, met in Lagos on Monday and reiterated its call for the constitution of an Interim National Government (ING) as a replacement for the incumbent administration of President Umaru Yar'Adua. Charles Ajunwa who witnessed the event, reports
Since August 14, this year, when the Nigerians United for Democracy (NUD), a conglomerate of political parties, civil society and self-determination groups first called for the constitution of an Interim National Government (ING) to replace the administration of President Umaru Yar'Adua, all has not been at ease in the polity.

Original_Search_Title: Where are Our Wise Men?
Headline: Nigeria: Where are Our Wise Men?
Body_Text:
My heartfelt cry for Nigeria today is the question: where are our wise men and women? I ask this question because never before in this country has there been this feeling of national despair, this palpable feeling of dejection and hopelessness.
No one right now is feeling upbeat about Nigeria; people no longer hold any hope of a better tomorrow because there is simply no indication to inspire such expectation. The conversation you hear these days is that nothing is working. And indeed there is no sphere of our national life that you can see that some progress is being made. Nigerians now adopt a wait-and- see attitude. Everybody is waiting and expecting a miracle from God. And miracle cannot happen without some positive actions.

Original_Search_Title: Economy Weighing on Voters' Minds
Headline: Angola: Economy Weighing on Voters' Minds
Body_Text:
Luanda — Angola's economy may be booming on the back of high oil prices and strong diamond exports, but six years after a peace deal ended the 27-year civil war, unemployment stands at around 65 percent.
Take a walk down any street in Luanda and next to the shiny banks and new office buildings, there are street vendors, many just teenagers, hawking anything they can find to make a living.

Original_Search_Title: Police Condemn Mob Justice
Headline: Botswana: Police Condemn Mob Justice
Body_Text:
The only time a thief is relieved at the sight of a police officer is when he or she is subjected to mob justice.
Police officers have often rescued criminals when members of the public were just about to finish them.

Original_Search_Title: Why Most of the Continent Remains Poor
Headline: Africa: Why Most of the Continent Remains Poor
Body_Text:
Kigali — The World Bank Tuesday released a report that indicates an increase in poverty levels across the world. The new report indicates that more people live in poverty compared to what had been earlier thought to be the case.
The report shows that 1.4 billion people in the world live in poverty up from 1 billion when the last study was conducted.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Tours Model Farm in Kanungu
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Tours Model Farm in Kanungu
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE instability in the Democratic Republic of Congo will not spill over into Uganda, the President said yesterday.
The fighting groups in Congo, he said, had no capacity to destabilise Uganda.

Original_Search_Title: President Says Djibouti Agreement 'Violated'
Headline: Somalia: President Says Djibouti Agreement 'Violated'
Body_Text:
Mogadishu — Somalia's transitional government Abdullahi Yusuf Ahmed has announced that there are elements violated the agreement of Djibouti that jointly signed by his Gov and the alliance for the reliberaion of Somalia, the major opposition group.
"There are groups those geared up for fight, disgusted peace, they rejected the last inclusive agreement of Djibouti" Yusuf said in a news conference he held in Mogadishu.

Original_Search_Title: They Face the Knife in Life And Death
Headline: Uganda: They Face the Knife in Life And Death
Body_Text:
For the Gisu men, circumcision is not an option as it is one practice they cannot escape even in death.
Heavy clouds hang precariously above Mutoto Bamasaba cultural ground on August 11. Several hours had passed since Bamasaba; hailing from Mbale, Manafwa, Bududa and Sironko had descended on this ground to launch the 2008 Imbalu circumcision ceremony.

Original_Search_Title: Country Yet to Tap Global Fruits Market Adequately
Headline: Uganda: Country Yet to Tap Global Fruits Market Adequately
Body_Text:
Kampala — Uganda has not adequately utilised the potential it has to tap into the US$1.2 billion global fruits market, a market information brief by the Uganda Export Promotions Board (UEPB) for the month of August has indicated.
Opportunities lie in exportation of organic avocadoes, mangoes & guavas, pineapples and yellow bananas.

Original_Search_Title: Film Workers Moan Over Low Pay
Headline: Namibia: Film Workers Moan Over Low Pay
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Alleged salary discrimination by a South African film company against Namibian film workers on a television series currently being shot on location at the coast, ended in an urgent consultative meeting between a number of aggrieved workers with the Permanent Secretary of Home Affairs.
The company, Out of Africa Entertainment South Africa, declined an invitation to attend the meeting that at times became highly charged due to many other complaints the workers are apparently daily facing on the set of the television series, The Prisoner.

Original_Search_Title: ACP Banana - PM Inoni Urges For More Competitiveness
Headline: Cameroon: ACP Banana - PM Inoni Urges For More Competitiveness
Body_Text:
Prime Minister Ephraim Inoni yesterday received a delegation of banana producers from the African, Caribbean and Pacific countries and Europe.
Cameroon's Prime Minister, Ephraim Inoni says, "It is not enough to protect banana but we must let our producers know that the world is competitive and that they must prepare to face the competition". He made the statement yesterday in Yaounde as he received members of the delegation of banana producers from the African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) countries, Association of European Banana Producers and the Dominican Republic.

Original_Search_Title: ACP Countries Reiterate Equal Market Access
Headline: Cameroon: ACP Countries Reiterate Equal Market Access
Body_Text:
Delegates issue Yaounde call after two-day meeting.
The banana war provoked by the decision of the European Union/Latin American banana producing countries to gradually reduce the EU tariff of 176 Euros per tonne to 116 Euros by 2015 entered a new phase following the meeting in Yaounde of delegates from banana producing African, Caribbean and Pacific Countries.

Original_Search_Title: Industrial Production Records 12.9 Percent Growth
Headline: Cameroon: Industrial Production Records 12.9 Percent Growth
Body_Text:
Cameroon's industrial sector has registered an increase production of 4.6 percent during 2007, GICAM quarterly Bulletin has said. The report published recently in Douala attributes the increase to the high demand for manufactured goods which pushed manufacturing industries to produce more. According to the bulletin, there was equally an increase in the demand for petroleum products.
In all, industrial activities in 2007 registered a growth rate of 12.9 percent up from 8.3 the year before, the Douala based bulleting of the Cameroon Association of Businessmen (Groupement Patronat du Cameroun-GICAM) said. The turnover for industries progressed from 7.3 percent in 2006 to 7.9 percent in 2007. The Economic and Financial Report for 2007 on its part stated inter alia that Cameroon's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) could jump to 4.1percent in 2008 up from 2.9 percent the previous year. All things remaining equal, manufacturing industries are expected to contribute 2.4 percent to the GDP against 2.1percent in 2007, GICAM report said.

Original_Search_Title: ACP Countries Consolidate Stand
Headline: Africa: ACP Countries Consolidate Stand
Body_Text:
Delegates of the African, Caribbean and Pacific Countries have declared their intension to send a hand of fellowship to their counterparts of Central and Latin America so they sit on one negotiating table in order to reach a compromise that can be endorsed by the competent authorities of the World Trade Organisation and put an end to the long drawn so called banana war.
The delegates who include Eric Delucci, president of the Guadeloupe and Martinique banana producers and Vice president of the European Banana Producer Association, Alfred Almond, Member of Parliament for Martinique and Mayor of Schoelcher, Emmanuel Jean-Michel, vice president of the Guadeloupe producer group and delegates from three African countries, Cameroon, Ivory Coast and Ghana among others, are meeting in Yaounde to rehearse their stand ahead of the next negotiation. In effect, after failing to strike a deal at the last WTO negotiations in Geneva, ACP countries want to reiterate their position on the decision of the European Union, the major consumers of Latin American banana to gradually reduce the EU's tariff of 176 Euros per tonne to 116 Euros by 2015. Cameroon's Minister of Trade, Luc Magloire Mbarga Atangana, who doubles as the spoke person for the African, Caribbean and Pacific countries on banana issues, staged a walk out during the meeting to express the disgruntlement and disagreement of the ACP countries on the proposal.

Original_Search_Title: How Ubudehe Changed a Man's Life in Ngoma District
Headline: Rwanda: How Ubudehe Changed a Man's Life in Ngoma District
Body_Text:
Kigali — Though most people have tried in vain to kick poverty out of their lives; Frecian Harerimana, has a unique story to tell.
In 2005, the government of Rwanda introduced a traditional assistance programme called Ubudehe, which operates in the country's lower administrative and cell levels.

Original_Search_Title: Golden Boy's Mother Couldn't Pay School Fees
Headline: Kenya: Golden Boy's Mother Couldn't Pay School Fees
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Newly crowned Olympic men marathon champion Samuel Kamau Wanjiru, and his brother joined Standard One two years late because their mother could not raise the building funds demanded by the school.
"Because of the struggle he saw his mother go through to see them attend primary school, Wanjiru came to revere his mother's name so much that he takes a lot of pride in calling himself Samuel Wanjiru as opposed to Samuel Kamau," said his uncle Peter Gichuhi.

Original_Search_Title: Re - Teenage Pregnancies And Sale of Babies
Headline: Nigeria: Re - Teenage Pregnancies And Sale of Babies
Body_Text:
MANY readers felt very strongly about the above topic and reactions flowed steadily in throughout the week.
Some are of the opinion that governments at all levels are not doing enough for the youths, and it is time for them to be alive to their responsibilities before the entire social structure collapses and the nation comes under siege.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Hopes the Best for Mengo
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Hopes the Best for Mengo
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has observed that if Mengo was ideologically smart, it would have realised by now that the Government has not pushed for harsher land reforms to have land re-allocated from landlords to the landless tenants.
The President said he enjoys the differences between his government and Mengo, the seat of Buganda Kingdom. Presently, the Government and Buganda are having differences over the Land Amendment Bill.

Original_Search_Title: President Isaias Inaugurates Banana And Tomato Processing Factory in Haikota Sub-Zone
Headline: Eritrea: President Isaias Inaugurates Banana And Tomato Processing Factory in Haikota Sub-Zone
Body_Text:
Haikota — President Isaias Afwerki yesterday inaugurated the newly set up Banana and Tomato Processing Factory in Alebu, Haikota sub-zone. The President observed the activities of the factory "Banatom," during which he received briefings by experts and managers.
Speaking at the inauguration ceremony in which ministers, regional Administrators, Army commanders, village elders and invited guests took part, the Minister of Trade and Industry, Dr. Gergis Teklemichael, indicated that the factory would be engaged in processing bananas over eight months and four months in processing tomatoes.

Original_Search_Title: Kagimu Advises Buganda on Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Kagimu Advises Buganda on Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — ECONOMIC monitoring state minister Morris Peter Kagimu Kiwanuka has warned Buganda Kingdom against using force while dealing with President Yoweri Museveni.
"If there is anything you want from President Museveni, you must handle him with care. Deal with him gently and you will get everything you need from him," Kagimu told Buganda Katikkiro John Baptist Walusimbi in Masaka on Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: Kaberamaido to Get Electricity
Headline: Uganda: Kaberamaido to Get Electricity
Body_Text:
Kaberamaido — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has said Kaberamaido and Katakwi districts will be connected to the national electricity power grid through Opuyo power station in Soroti.
The President yesterday said: "I have information that the funds are available but I cannot open the files now because of this rain.

Original_Search_Title: Chimp Marks Milestone
Headline: Tanzania: Chimp Marks Milestone
Body_Text:
A permanent building sits fairly isolated within a semi-natural environment, in a neatly trimmed compound. The doors are proper and the windows firmly fitted with wire mesh. It could pass for a home of a middle class Ugandan, except the neighbourhood is surrounded by a jungle, feathered with tree canopies and grass.
That is home for 11 chimpanzees now living in captivity at the Uganda Wildlife Education Centre (Uwec) in Entebbe. Last Friday was a special moment for the chimps as Zakayo; the alpha male and oldest-known chimpanzee living in captivity, celebrated his 44th birthday.

Original_Search_Title: High Food Prices Seeing No End
Headline: Ghana: High Food Prices Seeing No End
Body_Text:
A combination of scarce cash and higher prices of goods are deterring consumers from buying more items than they did two months ago, a cross-section of traders in Madina, one of the capital's ever-growing market centres, complained.
Speaking separately with Public Agenda just before the weekend, majority of the traders claimed they sold fewer goods in August than June 2008 or even the corresponding period of 2007.

Original_Search_Title: Boks Will Bounce Back, Says De Villiers After Nightmare Game
Headline: South Africa: Boks Will Bounce Back, Says De Villiers After Nightmare Game
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — OPTIMISTIC Springbok coach Peter de Villiers refused to wallow in sorrow after being brought down to earth with a bang in the Tri-Nations against New Zealand at the weekend and said his team had the character to bounce back.
The Springboks' Tri-Nations hopes were dealt a blow when they were thumped 19-0 by a determined All Blacks side at Newlands, who were hell-bent on avenging their 30-28 defeat in Dunedin a few weeks ago.

Original_Search_Title: A Portrait
Headline: Uganda: A Portrait
Body_Text:
Nairobi — SOME OF THE MOST MEMORAble pictures of Uganda from the early colonial days can be found in a new book published on the centenary celebration of the Italian Duke of the Abruzzi's expedition of Mount Rwenzori in 1906.
Some of the photographs were taken in 1906 by Vittorio Sella, during the Duke of the Abruzzi's expedition and others were taken a year later by Jan Czekanowski, the first professional anthropologist to visit the Rwenzori region when he was a member of the Duke of Mecklenburg's expedition.

Original_Search_Title: Eastleigh Goes Global
Headline: Kenya: Eastleigh Goes Global
Body_Text:
Nairobi — You can get anything you need here--even human body parts!" So I was told during my first visit to Eastleigh.
The year was 1979, and even then, Eastleigh was somehow different from other Nairobi mitaa (neighbourhoods). The streets were cluttered but non-threatening. Unpretentious kiosks served up excellent food to the sound of exotic music.

Original_Search_Title: To Sort Out Mess, Finding Competent Head Teachers is First Step
Headline: Uganda: To Sort Out Mess, Finding Competent Head Teachers is First Step
Body_Text:
Kampala — In the late 1990s, when many of the government-aided secondary schools were starting to face the heat from privately owned ones, a man named Fagil Mandy was the scourge of the upcountry schools.
As the Ministry of Education's top officer in charge of inspection, Mr Mandy earned his notoriety through his tough tactics with head teachers, especially if he found them inept.

Original_Search_Title: 'Matatu Presidency' And Power Vacuum
Headline: Kenya: 'Matatu Presidency' And Power Vacuum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki says there is no leadership vacuum in Kenya. And I agree.
Instead of a vacuum, there is a leadership saturation. We have a bloated cabinet and a 'matatu presidency'.With power sharing, the presidency has been split into two.

Original_Search_Title: Quite a Vibrant Metropolis
Headline: Namibia: Quite a Vibrant Metropolis
Body_Text:
Beijing, China — During the day and at night, a mass of unflinching, motionless soldiers dressed in immaculately starched military attire stand ramrod- stiff routinely scanning the hustle and bustle of this city of Beijing teeming with a population of almost 20 million people.
And on top of the resident population of 18 million or so, there are millions of tourists attending the Beijing 2008 Olympics co-hosted by Tianjin and Qindao along the coast.

Original_Search_Title: Bright Heights of Hope in the Gloom
Headline: South Africa: Bright Heights of Hope in the Gloom
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — BRILLIANT columnist and academic the late Dr Anthony Holiday prophesied that under Thabo Mbeki's rule, SA could become the banana of banana republics.
The Economist's recent succinct account of the internecine power struggle within the African National Congress (ANC) makes SA look like a serious competitor for the Guinness Book of Records as basket case of the world.

Original_Search_Title: Ribadu's Double Demotion - Matters Arising
Headline: Nigeria: Ribadu's Double Demotion - Matters Arising
Body_Text:
Lagos — When Michael Okiro, Inspector-General of Police (IGP), boasted that he and he alone decided on sending Nuhu Ribadu, a presidential appointee like himself, to NIPSS, I wondered aloud if Nigeria had become a banana republic.
I made my view known in an interview granted to a reporter of this newspaper in January 2008. Subsequently, the Communication Adviser to the president confirmed that Okiro took the decision without prior consultation with the president!

Original_Search_Title: Mbale to Get Industrial Estate
Headline: Uganda: Mbale to Get Industrial Estate
Body_Text:
Kampala — Business came to a standstill in Mbale town as crowds jammed the routes to Mbale Stadium to listen to President Yoweri Museveni launching a plan to reduce unemployment, especially among the youth in urban centres.
During his Prosperity-for-All (Bona Bagaggawale) campaign in Bugisu sub-region on Tuesday, Museveni said Mbale was one of the 22 zones where industrial estates would be built to create more jobs for the youth.

Original_Search_Title: 50 Murdered in Mbarara
Headline: Uganda: 50 Murdered in Mbarara
Body_Text:
Kampala — A TOTAL of 51 people have been killed in Mbarara district since the year began, the Police have said.
The crime chief, Jackson Tumwine, on Monday told journalists at the Central Police Station that six of the murders were committed in January, seven in February, nine in March and another seven in April.

Original_Search_Title: Matooke Flour Production Starts
Headline: Uganda: Matooke Flour Production Starts
Body_Text:
Kampala — The first batch of flour made out of bananas, commonly known as matooke, was launched in Kampala yesterday.
The brands are Raw Tooke Flour and the Instant Tooke Flour.

Original_Search_Title: Stop Wrangles, Museveni Warns NRM Leaders
Headline: Uganda: Stop Wrangles, Museveni Warns NRM Leaders
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Yoweri Museveni has warned the National Resistance Movement leaders to stop wrangling over who should be the party leader.
Museveni said everyone in the NRM leadership should focus on how to solve problems facing citizens.

Original_Search_Title: Crack Down on Dodgy Fuel Pumps
Headline: Rwanda: Crack Down on Dodgy Fuel Pumps
Body_Text:
Kigali — The Rwanda Bureau of Standards (RBS), a government agency for ensuring standards of products, is cracking down on substandard fuel pumps.
According to Gilbert Barigye, an RBS official, the exercise began with the verification of the quality of fuel pumps in Kigali city centre last week but will proceed countrywide.

Original_Search_Title: Briefcase Businesses Take Root
Headline: Uganda: Briefcase Businesses Take Root
Body_Text:
Kampala — Because of the advancement in technology and cut-throat competition, briefcase businesses are coming up as well as virtual offices.
Timothy Bukumunhe writes that one doesn't need to own a physical office to make money. A mobile phone and or a hotspot will suffice

Original_Search_Title: My Experience
Headline: Malawi: My Experience
Body_Text:
BLANTYRE: The flashy and fancy flight that Malawi operates totally contradicts the status of the airport in Blantyre. In fact, the airport is a true reflection of the city's economic status.
Perhaps, it was because I had expected more than what the town has to offer. I imagined a beautiful town with skyscrapers, dual carriageways, roundabouts, traffic lights and fancy cars dominating good road networks, hence I could not resist the urge to see myself in the central business district (CBD) of this town with an English name, Blantyre (named after David Livingstone's birth place in Scotland).

Original_Search_Title: Tafi Atome Monkey Sanctuary - an Ecotourism Success Story
Headline: Ghana: Tafi Atome Monkey Sanctuary - an Ecotourism Success Story
Body_Text:
Wooden signs point down well-maintained paths into the bush at the Tafi Atome Monkey Sanctuary in the Volta Region near Hohoe. The trails wind into the bush where hundreds of Mona monkeys spy on visitors from high above in branches. The sanctuary allows tourists to directly interact with Mona monkeys.
Before the sanctuary was established in 1993, the local population hunted the Mona monkey for its meat. But now, the monkeys are revered and protected and the sanctuary provides a stream of revenue into the area. Foster Asempa, one of the four tour guides at the sanctuary, said that Tafi Atome generates about 10,000 cedis for the local community a year. And that money goes to fund development projects in Tafi Atome, where 1,500 people live.

Original_Search_Title: COTA to Address Art Diploma Confusion
Headline: Namibia: COTA to Address Art Diploma Confusion
Body_Text:
Windhoek — A confrontation between the management of the College of the Arts and two lecturers of the African Performing Arts Department last week resulted in serious criminal accusations of defamation and character assassination.
A furious Banana Shekupe, the head of the department, revealed to Art/Life that he was very unhappy about a threatening letter in which he and fellow music lecturer, Dominic Lunenge, were summarily summoned to appear before the management committee of COTA.

Original_Search_Title: Dismas Nkunda - Both Uganda And Buganda Have a Point
Headline: Uganda: Dismas Nkunda - Both Uganda And Buganda Have a Point
Body_Text:
There is an adage that when two elephants fight it's the grass that suffers. We can add a rejoinder; that even if the elephants were making love, the grass would still suffer.
The anger that led President Museveni to order the arrest of the Buganda trio must be deep seated and strong. I do not claim to listen to all the programmes on CBS radio, but the whispers from those who listen in is good fodder for thought. Now that the three officials have been released, I have a question which might not have clear answers; what does the arrest of the trio mean to a lone eye?

Original_Search_Title: Lazy Contractors to Face Prosecution
Headline: Nigeria: Lazy Contractors to Face Prosecution
Body_Text:
Contractors handling various development projects in Ebonyi State have been warned to complete their contracts on schedule or risk being prosecuted in the court of law.
Ebonyi State Governor, Chief Martin Elechi, issued this stern warning to government contractors in the state when National Assembly members from the state paid him a courtesy visit at the government House, Abakaliki.

Original_Search_Title: Prosperity Plan Has Boosted Farmers Through Model Farms
Headline: Uganda: Prosperity Plan Has Boosted Farmers Through Model Farms
Body_Text:
Kampala — JOAN Kakwenzire is the head of the Poverty Alleviation Department at State House.
Cyprian Musoke interviewed her on what progress the Government has registered in its Prosperity-for-All (Bonna Bagaggawale) campaign.

Original_Search_Title: The Rule of Loot
Headline: Nigeria: The Rule of Loot
Body_Text:
"When values are sufficient, laws are unnecessary. When values are insufficient, laws are unenforceable." - Barry Asmus
I have often stated on these pages that Nigerians, as a collective, have never been more in need of the services of psychiatric help than at this material time in their history. Few can also deny that at no other time in our evolution as a sovereign entity have our leaders and elites demonstrated so much arrogance or exhibited the present degree of monumental contempt for our feelings and collective aspirations.

Original_Search_Title: Seven Die in Kabale Accident
Headline: Uganda: Seven Die in Kabale Accident
Body_Text:
Kampala — Two more people have died at Kabale Referral Hospital making the death toll seven and over 40 sustained serious injuries when a bus registration number UAG 373Y belonging to Gateway bus Company heading for Kampala from Kisoro overturned at Bukinda trading centre just 20km from Kabale town along the Kabale Mbarara-Kampala highway on Saturday.
The bus knocked down a woman resident of the same area identified as Jackline Niwagaba together with her two children only identified as Daisy and Praise. The trio were reportedly walking on the road side proceeding to a nearby Seventh day Adventist church for prayers when the bus rammed into a nearby house after knocking them.

Original_Search_Title: DO, Mayor Implicate Lapiro in Mbanga Unrest
Headline: Cameroon: DO, Mayor Implicate Lapiro in Mbanga Unrest
Body_Text:
The Divisional Officer, DO, for Mbanga Subdivision, Simon Nkwenti, Wednesday, July 23 at the Nkongsamba High Court testified that Pierre Roger Lambo Sandjo aka Lapiro de Mbanga, rallied boys who caused the February strike in Mbanga.
Nkwenti was speaking at the court during the trial of the 51-year-old musician.After Lapiro pleaded not guilty, Nkwenti said that on February 25 when the unrest began, he made frantic efforts to get Lapiro on phone.

Original_Search_Title: Olympic Gold - Team Spirit Will Be Key
Headline: Nigeria: Olympic Gold - Team Spirit Will Be Key
Body_Text:
Olympic football gold medallist Garba Lawal says Dream Team IV will need togetherness and a healthy dose of luck if they are to go all the way and win the gold medal.
Lawal, a member of the original Dream Team which won gold in Atlanta in 1996, told KickOffNigeria.com that apart from the talent in that squad, team spirit and luck won Nigeria the gold medal. "They have to have team work, team spirit and like each other, otherwise its going to be difficult," Lawal advised."If you want to be champion, you have to like each other and that is what we had when we won it in 1996.

Original_Search_Title: Who Are These People?
Headline: Uganda: Who Are These People?
Body_Text:
Kampala — THEY have been in the news for a week. But who are the three Mengo officials who have been charged with inciting violence and terrorism?
If jail was a ticket to power, Betty Nambooze Bakireke would, perhaps, be the most powerful politician by now. The Democratic Party's right-hand woman has been in and out of cells countless times. In almost all cases, her arrest has been linked to what she says.

Original_Search_Title: COTA Management Looks for Scapegoats
Headline: Namibia: COTA Management Looks for Scapegoats
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Two lecturers were yesterday summoned to appear before the management committee of the College of the Arts allegedly as scapegoats for the institution's obvious inability to resolve the issue of non-accreditation of four courses with the National Qualification Authority.
Yesterday morning emotionally charged students of the African Performing Arts Department were gathered outside the meeting venue in solidarity with the two lecturers.

Original_Search_Title: Olympic Gold - Team Spirit Will Be Key
Headline: Nigeria: Olympic Gold - Team Spirit Will Be Key
Body_Text:
Olympic football gold medallist Garba Lawal says Dream Team IV will need togetherness and a healthy dose of luck if they are to go all the way and win the gold medal.
Lawal, a member of the original Dream Team which won gold in Atlanta in 1996, told KickOffNigeria.com that apart from the talent in that squad, team spirit and luck won Nigeria the gold medal.

Original_Search_Title: The Withering of Memory and History
Headline: Nigeria: The Withering of Memory and History
Body_Text:
Abuja — A few events of recent days have brought forcefully to the front burner of my consciousness the relevance of memory and of history.
At the weekend, my daughter, Innawuro, asked me when I was going to take her and her siblings on a visit to our ancestral home, Wurno, in Sokoto State. What kind of place was it? Why did our folks leave that place all those years ago?

Original_Search_Title: Senate - Mark's New Plans And Lurking Dangers
Headline: Nigeria: Senate - Mark's New Plans And Lurking Dangers
Body_Text:
Lagos — In the senate, the end of one political drama is the beginning of another.
Though the leadership of the senate and indeed the senators had said that the era of political' banana peel' in the Upper House was over, there is always what many now call present and clear danger at the nation's apex lawmaking body.

Original_Search_Title: Teachers Rampant, Gambari's Shame, Mark's Alright
Headline: Nigeria: Teachers Rampant, Gambari's Shame, Mark's Alright
Body_Text:
Those who assert that the teaching profession has gone full cycle are more correct than those who say that for education.
