The former are the people who know what is really at play within the core of the teachers' strike.

Original_Search_Title: Mugabe And Africa's Habit of Unhappiness
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mugabe And Africa's Habit of Unhappiness
Body_Text:
You may say that it is Mongo Beti who wrote a novel titled "Perpetua and the habit of unhappiness". We know. But do you know what "strongmen" and "banana republics" are?
They are "defining" characteristics of Africa! The story goes that usually monkeys fight in the jungle for bananas until the strong monkeys have them for themselves. Since in Africa there are life and death struggles for power until the "strongmen" grab it, our countries can be referred to as "banana republics"!

Original_Search_Title: Minister Wabudeya Hails Mpigi Anti-Poverty Drive
Headline: Uganda: Minister Wabudeya Hails Mpigi Anti-Poverty Drive
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Poverty Alleviation Fund programmes have improved the lives of residents of Muduma sub-county in Mpigi district, the minister for the presidency has said.
Dr. Beatrice Wabudeya made the remarks on Tuesday while touring projects set up using the funds. She visited 12 homesteads in Nakyesanje, Bulamu and Galabi parishes to assess whether the money had been properly used.

Original_Search_Title: What Are Kampala's Posh Hotels Selling?
Headline: Uganda: What Are Kampala's Posh Hotels Selling?
Body_Text:
Kampala — IF you build it, they will come. The quote is credited to the 26th US president, Theodore Roosevelt Jr. at the time of the building of the Panama Canal in 1904. I thought about this quote as I strolled along Speke Road in Kampala last Thursday afternoon.
Starting from the older and greyer Grand Imperial and Speke hotels, I walked past the regally imposing and renovated Sheraton, toward the Serena hotel, turning slightly left onto the spectacularly crafted work of poetry that is the Grand Imperial Royale Hotel.

Original_Search_Title: 888 Days in Biafra (17)
Headline: Nigeria: 888 Days in Biafra (17)
Body_Text:
We were marched into the yard to await further instructions. Adeola was a polished man. He had seen me but did not want to get me into trouble with the warders by calling my attention. When at last my searching eyes went to his direction and saw him, my heart leapt for joy and I shouted "Egbon" ("Big brother"). He motioned to me to be quiet and that he would see me later. His cell looked like one of the single cells at Okigwe and I wondered when and where he was going to see me again unless we were locked up together. He looked lean but quite healthy and full of smiles. My war ended immediately I saw him. I instantly felt at ease and at peace.
"D Ward" at Umuahia was originally used as the prison store for the Records Department. When it was decided to evacuate us to Umuahia, the store was cleaned out and a six foot high palm-leaves fence erected at a distance of 12ft around the building. The main wooden beams of the fence had been completed before our arrival. We needed no ghost to tell us that that would be our cell. What was left was for the detailed prisoners to fill in the wooden mesh with palm leaves and for the carpenter to fit a strong door. Umuahia Prison itself is small compared with all other federal or Biafran make-shift-prisons. The only exception, perhaps, could be Broad Street Prisons Lagos. I understand that when it was first constructed the original intention was for it to serve the purpose of a temporary prison for "awaiting trials" and first offenders that came to serve sentences of short durations. It was also meant to accommodate prisoners from other centres, who came to answer further charges against them in cases pending at Umuahia courts. All told, Umuahia Prison was not meant to accommodate more than 60 inmates and as such the station was headed by a Sergeant.

Original_Search_Title: Zima Award Winner Garakara Diabetic
Headline: Zimbabwe: Zima Award Winner Garakara Diabetic
Body_Text:
Harare — SUNGURA musician, Joseph Garakara of the "Idya banana" fame has revealed that he is diabetic.
This followed a diagnosis three months ago.

Original_Search_Title: Vital Omission in the Budget!
Headline: Uganda: Vital Omission in the Budget!
Body_Text:
Kampala — Looking back at Dr Ezra Suruma's budget read on June 14, I am not sure I give him kudos like most Ugandans did in the subsequent press.
There was one fundamental omission in the last budget - it excluded analysis of budgetary performance for the previous year. Uganda should borrow a leaf from Kenya, which adopted performance contracts for mainstream Government and public enterprises. The public functions are assessed and scored, against the pledges made in the performance contracts for the previous year, before allocation of funds in the next financial year.

Original_Search_Title: Zimbabwe Needs a Political Settlement
Headline: Zimbabwe Needs a Political Settlement
Body_Text:
It is common knowledge that the Movement of Democratic Change (MDC) party won the parliamentary and presidential elections earlier this year. Based on its performance, it would therefore be fair to say that the MDC would probably have also won last week's presidential run-off had it not pulled out at the last moment. Yet, despite these facts, Zanu-PF still remains in power today. Robert Mugabe has once again outmaneuvered his opponents in Zimbabwe and abroad.
No surprises there then, given that the government had set in motion a chain of events that were designed to pre-determine the outcome of the elections in its favour. Indeed, the MDC cited the systematic harassment, torture and murder of its supporters and leadership as the main reason for its withdrawal from the election.

Original_Search_Title: Daniel Denies Plot to Impeach Speaker
Headline: Nigeria: Daniel Denies Plot to Impeach Speaker
Body_Text:
Abuja — Ogun State Governor, Governor Gbenga Daniel, has denied speculations that he is lobbying to impeach Speaker of the House of Assembly, Mr Tunji Egbekotun over complicity in the removal of his predecessor, Mrs Titi Oseni.
Crises between the state executive and the legislature, which has been on for a while, took another turn with the recent allegation of attempted murder of the governor through food poisoning.

Original_Search_Title: No More Crisis in Ogun - Daniel
Headline: Nigeria: No More Crisis in Ogun - Daniel
Body_Text:
Abuja — Ogun State Governor, Otunba Gbenga Daniel said yesterday in Abuja that the political crisis rocking the state has been resolved, saying there was never any move by the State House of Assembly to impeach him.
Governor Daniel told State House correspondents that "Ogun is fine, peaceful. We had some challenges in the House of Assembly. A lot of misinformation by interested parties, outside parties, people who are usually interested when there is crisis. But Ogun State remains one of the most peaceful states in Nigeria .

Original_Search_Title: Why I Will Vote for Museveni 2011
Headline: Uganda: Why I Will Vote for Museveni 2011
Body_Text:
Given what has been happening ever since the selections in Kenya last December, I have decided to become wise.
Even if he does not mention that he will stand in 2011, I will vote for him. My reasons are based on what has happened in Kenya and Zimbabwe. This will be my only patriotic duty.

Original_Search_Title: The Man Who Loves to Baby-Sit Wild Animals
Headline: Uganda: The Man Who Loves to Baby-Sit Wild Animals
Body_Text:
Kampala — Michael Ocen has loved animals since he was a young boy. Born 43 years ago in Pader district, Ocen's love for animals is so strong that he will not even eat the meat of cows or goats slaughtered in his presence. "I eat meat but not if the cow is slaughtered as I watch. My love for animals has even driven me into quarrels with people I don't know," he says.
Ocen hated the hunters in his village who used to go out to kill the animals he loved so much. "I could not eat the meat the hunters brought home."

Original_Search_Title: Britain Rejects Mugabe Control of Unity Govt
Headline: Zimbabwe: Britain Rejects Mugabe Control of Unity Govt
Body_Text:
An interview with British Foreign Secretary David Miliband with BBC Radio 4's World at One. Miliband was in Johannesburg.
Let me ask you first of all if I may about this recurring of, of violence within Zimbabwe, do you think that Mugabe's forces are behind the recent attacks?

Original_Search_Title: Is Kimunya a Victim of Intrigues?
Headline: Kenya: Is Kimunya a Victim of Intrigues?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Regarding Mr Amos Kimunya, I submit that the country is behaving badly. Our aggression on the man is displaced, cowardly and excessive.
What is worse: we have not examined the evidence, and we are not even interested. That is why when we discuss the matter, all we can do is exchange ignorance.

Original_Search_Title: State House Defends Amelia Kyambadde
Headline: Uganda: State House Defends Amelia Kyambadde
Body_Text:
Kampala — STATE House has dismissed allegations that the Principal Private Secretary to the President, Amelia Kyambadde, and former MP Mutebi Kityo received sh125m and sh113m respectively from the Poverty Alleviation Department (PAD).
Senior Presidential Advisor PAD, Joan Kakwenzire, told journalists on Friday that the allegations were "a distortion of facts intended to incite the public and discredit the Presidential initiatives against hunger and poverty." She cited Nakyessanja model village on Mityana road as one of the initiatives.

Original_Search_Title: Mob Lynches Man Over Cannibalism
Headline: Uganda: Mob Lynches Man Over Cannibalism
Body_Text:
Kampala — ANGRY residents of Kinaawa village in Dwaniro sub-county, Rakai district, on Tuesday lynched a 70-year-old man suspected to be a cannibal, reports Ali Mambule.
Lekobwami Ssozi was beaten with stones and clubs before his body was set ablaze, while his son, Samuel Batte, was only saved by the Police who shot in the air to disperse the mob.

Original_Search_Title: Barrister Enonchong Dies
Headline: Cameroon: Barrister Enonchong Dies
Body_Text:
The earthly journey for the ebullient Barrister at Law came to a stop Thursday, June 26 at the Polyclinic Muna in Bonanjo, Douala. Family sources told this reporter His Majesty, Chief, Dr. Henry Ndifor Abi Enonchong suffered from a severe cardiac arrest.
Born March 22, 1934 in his native Besongabang in Manyu Division, Southwest Province, Enonchong is the direct descendant of the ancestor, Chief Abane, said to be the founder of the Besongabang chiefdom, whose father was Ayuktayak.

Original_Search_Title: Nass Not Probing Anybody - Mark
Headline: Nigeria: Nass Not Probing Anybody - Mark
Body_Text:
Senate president, David Mark, has dispelled speculations and media reports that the National Assembly was probing past administration, saying that the legislature was merely conducting public hearings with a view to rectifying the mistakes of the past.
Mark, who is also the chairman of the National Assembly disclosed this in an exclusive interview with Leadership Sunday in Abuja.

Original_Search_Title: Little Hope for Cancer Patients in Country
Headline: Uganda: Little Hope for Cancer Patients in Country
Body_Text:
Kampala — AFTER giving birth by caesarean section last year, Hellen Musoke, felt a lump, the size of a banana, under her ribs. The doctors knew about it, but sent her home, saying it might be a bruise. In a week's time, Musoke was back to the hospital, terribly ill, swollen with fluid, vomiting and anaemic.
She needed a blood transfusion and suffered from severe abdominal pain. Tests revealed she had colon cancer that had spread to her liver and was now at stage four, the final phase of the disease.

Original_Search_Title: 'Am I Just Muzungu, Or' - Could We Be Doing a Little More?
Headline: Rwanda: 'Am I Just Muzungu, Or' - Could We Be Doing a Little More?
Body_Text:
Kigali — As a privileged middle-class kid growing up in New York City, I used to play a lot of video games. Most video games are obnoxious wastes of space, time and money, but some of them could be educational.
One such game, ranking amongst my life-long favorites, was a game called Sim City. I liked this game because it let you play God, and you could make whatever your dreams summoned, building terrains, towns, small villages or bustling cities. My favorite was bustling cities.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Outlines Investment Strategy
Headline: East Africa: Museveni Outlines Investment Strategy
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Yoweri Museveni yesterday cited peace, security, infrastructure and tax incentives as some of the key factors to improve the business climate in East Africa.
In a remarkable plea for private enterprise, the former Marxist revolutionary also called for less red tape and intervention by politicians and bureaucrats.

Original_Search_Title: Expert Proffers Solution to Demolition of Worship Places
Headline: Nigeria: Expert Proffers Solution to Demolition of Worship Places
Body_Text:
As various arms of  government continue to demolish worship buildings used by either Christians or Muslims, experts have identified the need to perfect land titles in order to protect and enhance such buildings.
Speaking with LEADERSHIP at a forum recently in Lagos , Mr. Abiodun Oluwaluyi, a Housing Delivery Director in the Lagos State Service, said most of the worship buildings, be it church or mosque lacks the required documentation to protect it against demolition.

Original_Search_Title: Democracy Day - Which Date is More Appropriate?
Headline: Nigeria: Democracy Day - Which Date is More Appropriate?
Body_Text:
Abuja — On May 29th 2008,Nigeria celebrated National Democracy Day. It was a public holiday and the 9th in the series. Former president Olusegun Obasanjo had instituted May 29th of every year as Democracy Day in Nigeria. This declaration has not gone unchallenged by the critical segments of the Nigerian society - the media, civil society and a section of the political class. These groups largely believe that May 29th does not eminently qualify as a democracy day for Nigeria.
They agreed that although it was the last terminal date of military rule in Nigeria, they insist that it was just a culmination of an arranged political process for a predetermined outcome; a product of a carefully crafted political process by the departing military political elite, in consultation with their civilian counterparts to appease a section of the country widely acknowledged to have been wronged by thoughtless annulment of the presidential election of June 12 1993- the freest, most peaceful and most credible election ever held in the history of Nigeria.

Original_Search_Title: The Budget Fell Short of Fundamentals
Headline: Uganda: The Budget Fell Short of Fundamentals
Body_Text:
Looking back at Minister Suruma's budget of June 12, I am not sure I should give him praises like most Ugandans did.
There was one fundamental omission in the budget-- it excluded analysis of budgetary performance for the previous year. Uganda should borrow a leaf from Kenya, which adopted performance contracts for mainstream government and public enterprises.

Original_Search_Title: Aga Khan Raises Charity Money With Car Wash
Headline: Uganda: Aga Khan Raises Charity Money With Car Wash
Body_Text:
June 13 was a remarkable moment for the pupils of Aga Khan Primary School as they actively participated in community service car wash project in a bid to raise funds for the completion of a library block at St Noah Primary School in Nabinene, Wakiso district. Parents and well-wishers brought their cars to be washed at a fee of Shs5,000.
The chairperson of the Aga Khan Community Service Club, Ms Christine Kasaato says it is through such kind hearts that the club has been able to make a difference in the lives of St. Noah's children.

Original_Search_Title: 2 Drown As Hippo Hits Boat
Headline: Namibia: 2 Drown As Hippo Hits Boat
Body_Text:
Windhoek — An adult and a baby drowned in flood waters between Schuksmansburg and Zambia, east of Katima Mulilo, on Wednesday.
Police in Windhoek reported yesterday that a banana boat was bumped by a hippo and the boat capsized. The body of the adult was recovered while that of the baby is still missing.

Original_Search_Title: I Run an Open Administration - Mark
Headline: Nigeria: I Run an Open Administration - Mark
Body_Text:
Lagos — How has it been presiding over the Senate of the Federal Republic of Nigeria for one year? Since I have the experience in the Senate before I became the Senate President, it does not appear strange to me, and it's not as if I'm just going there starting from the beginning to learn.
I believe in the last one year, the greatest thing that we have achieved in the Senate is stability. We had disagreement in some areas, we had some difficulties, but at the end of the day, we have been able to come up with a consensus and moved in the same frequency. We have achieved a lot. Of course, those who came in new have also become more conversant now with the process of law making.

Original_Search_Title: Tornado CC Face Party Poppers Kutchimadu
Headline: Uganda: Tornado CC Face Party Poppers Kutchimadu
Body_Text:
So much will be at stake for perennial underachievers Nile CC and giant killers Kutchimadu when the semifinals of the Mawanda Cup hog the limelight today.
Both sides upped the tempo of their preparations for the crunch semifinal duels and are optimistic about their chances of causing some upsets.

Original_Search_Title: 888 Days in Biafra (14)
Headline: Nigeria: 888 Days in Biafra (14)
Body_Text:
Apart from holding court sessions in the cells, Saturday nights were usually club nights. Feeding pans and bed-posts were used for providing music while others clapped hands. It was always a night of send-off parties for prisoners who would be discharged the following week. Apart from free tobacco, illicit gin has been known to have been smuggled into cells for use at the party.
A compromising warder could always have his own share as long as he turned a blind eye and allowed the "CROFO" or "KOROFO" (The provost of the cell) to take charge. As they rationalised it, the next day's meal was uncertain, but tonight "Man must whack". Our food situation was deteriorating fast. By May we were no longer sure of two rice meals a day. We could not swallow our pride and ask for garri. But soon we were tired of chewing rice and appealed to the cook warder to give us pounded rice with soup occasionally. The soup itself was bad enough but to swallow pounded rice with that was just the limit of pretence as far as I was concerned. I made my views known to the house and was surprised when I chalked up three other supporters. So while others had pounded rice, those in my gang had ordinary rice.

Original_Search_Title: Full Marks for Amodu, Eagles at Half-Way Stage
Headline: Nigeria/Angola: Full Marks for Amodu, Eagles at Half-Way Stage
Body_Text:
Lagos — The Super Eagles are having a real ball in the on going Angola/South Africa 2010 second round campaign, which hit the halfway mark at the weekend.
The Eagles' 1-0 win over minnows Equatorial Guinea in Malabo on Sunday has put Nigeria in pole position in Group 4 ranking it among the four countries in the entire nine group qualifying series with a 100 per cent results in qualifying.

Original_Search_Title: Key Road Cut Off By Heavy Rains
Headline: Kenya: Key Road Cut Off By Heavy Rains
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The road linking Mombasa and Garissa has been cut off after heavy rains in Coast Province destroyed some sections of the highway.
Thirty trucks and several buses have been stuck in the mud at Baomo, about 50km north of Garsen, for the last three days. The transport hitch has sparked fears of food shortage in Tana River District and some parts of North Eastern Province where the vehicles were to deliver supplies. Hundreds of passengers were inconvenienced and some had to travel to Garsen from where they boarded other vehicles to Malindi and Mombasa.

Original_Search_Title: Kagame Sees 'Opportunity' in Global Food Crisis
Headline: Rwanda: Kagame Sees 'Opportunity' in Global Food Crisis
Body_Text:
Kigali — Low investment and hoarding are to blame for the ongoing global food crisis which has very simple solutions - "more production and productivity", President Paul Kagame said on Wednesday.
Instead of countries acknowledging the problem of food by investing more into production, Mr. Kagame said they have been hoarding every produce they have thereby creating current shortfalls.

Original_Search_Title: 76 Graduate at St Peters
Headline: Gambia: 76 Graduate at St Peters
Body_Text:
St Peters Junior and Senior Secondary school, last Saturday, held its 28th annual graduation and prize giving ceremony, at the school grounds.
In his annual report, Rev father Bruno Toupan, the principal of the school, said they had worked hard over the years and had maintained high standard of excellence in teaching.

Original_Search_Title: Cami Toyota Official Wins CDC Golf Tournament
Headline: Cameroon: Cami Toyota Official Wins CDC Golf Tournament
Body_Text:
The Commercial Attaché of CAMI Toyota, Ignatius Bah, is this year's winner of the annual President's Putter Golf tournament organised by the Cameroon Development Corporation, CDC.
Bah, 38, won the coveted prize recently at the Likomba Golf Club, Tiko.The 2008 winner told The Post that it took him several week-ends of training at the Golf course, to emerge winner of.

Original_Search_Title: Yobo Strikes Again to Give Eagles Victory
Headline: Nigeria/Cote d'Ivoire: Yobo Strikes Again to Give Eagles Victory
Body_Text:
Lagos — Super Eagles defender Joseph Yobo scored again for the second consecutive weekend to secure victory for Nigeria against Equatorial Guinea in Malabo yesterday
Besides ensuring that the Super Eagles kept their unbeaten record intact but also ensured that Nigeria avoided the banana peel which consumed a number of big names over the weekend.

Original_Search_Title: Children Forced to Work As Poverty, HIV Bite
Headline: Uganda: Children Forced to Work As Poverty, HIV Bite
Body_Text:
Kampala — EVERY year, on June 16, the Day of the African Child is held, to commemorate the killing of 100 black students in South Africa in a protest against poor education. This year's theme is 'Right to participation: let children be heard and seen'. But, as Maria Wamala writes, the situation of children in Uganda is worrying, with 2.7million subjected to hazardous child labour
It is early in the morning, but 10-year-old Gerald Balyokweyo is already looking tired, pale and hungry. Carrying bundles of carrots and tomatoes in his small hands, he walks from Mengo Kisenyi to Kampala without breakfast.

Original_Search_Title: Mark's Tribunal Case and Stakes in the Senate
Headline: Nigeria: Mark's Tribunal Case and Stakes in the Senate
Body_Text:
Even before the Benue State Election Petition Tribunal cancelled the election of Senate President David Mark on February 23, some senators had been having clandestine meetings to pick a successor to Mark.
Mark is Nigeria's number three citizen by virtue of his position as Senate President. As a good student of history, Mark has since shown that he has learnt to remove the proverbial banana peel which was the albatross of many of his predecessors. Since his landslide victory over his kinsman, former Benue State Governor George Akume, Mark has won the support of his colleagues in the chamber with his style of leadership. But some of his colleagues were not swayed with that record as they gathered in several highly restrictive meetings around Abuja and even shut out senators who were already identified as 'Mark's people.'

Original_Search_Title: Rough Ride Home
Headline: Ethiopia: Rough Ride Home
Body_Text:
Nairobi — IT IS TUESDAY, MARCH 5, AT THE break of dawn in Addis Ababa. The sleepy city stirs to life.
Soon, residents of the city will be out and about in pursuit of their daily bread. I, too, must get up. I have a five-day road trip ahead of me.

Original_Search_Title: Land of Sisal Plantations
Headline: East Africa: Land of Sisal Plantations
Body_Text:
Kigali — If you have never travelled by land from Rwanda to the heart of the biggest country in East Africa, then get to know from Kelvin Odoobo's experience.
We reached Namanga, a small border town at the Kenya - Tanzania border at about two a.m. in the morning, completely rattled by the happenings a few hours before on the Nairobi - Mombassa highway.

Original_Search_Title: Another Letter to the Cranes
Headline: Uganda: Another Letter to the Cranes
Body_Text:
I know that by now, you have probably had enough from all corners and kinds of people. That is football and just like any other profession, there are good and bad times.
It's very hard to sustain successful football run unless you are willing to deal with the bad times. Everybody faces these bad times but the difference is in the way we deal with them, both as individuals and as a team. Like I said before, you should never fear criticism if you are a footballer because it is part of the job.

Original_Search_Title: Abacha, Akpamgbo Annulled June 12 - Nwosu
Headline: Nigeria: Abacha, Akpamgbo Annulled June 12 - Nwosu
Body_Text:
Lagos — CHAIRMAN of the defunct National Electoral Commission (NEC), Professor Humphrey Nwosu, has named the late Gen. Sani Abacha and one-time Attorney-General of the Federation, Chief Clement Akpamgbo, as the principal actors in the nullification of the June 12, 1993 election won by the late Chief M.K.O. Abiola.
Prof. Nwosu at the launch in Abuja yesterday of a book chronicling preparations and events that followed the election exonerated the then military President Ibrahim Babangida from the events that led to the annulment, saying Babangida gave him the strongest support for to the elections.

Original_Search_Title: The Republic of Uganda Budget Speech Financial Year 2008/09
Headline: Uganda: The Republic of Uganda Budget Speech Financial Year 2008/09
Body_Text:
Kampala — Exports increase by 15%
Theme: Strategic priorities to accelerate Prosperity- For-All

Original_Search_Title: Storage Facility Set Up At Eldoret Airport
Headline: Kenya: Storage Facility Set Up At Eldoret Airport
Body_Text:
Eldoret Airport has received a boost with the installation of a 240-tonne cold storage facility.
The facility, which was set up by Canken International, is expected to boost storage capacity at the airport as it seeks to become a cargo handling hub in East and Central Africa.

Original_Search_Title: M7 Lectures World Leaders
Headline: Uganda: M7 Lectures World Leaders
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Yoweri Museveni yesterday repeated his assertion before world leaders that the rising food prices were a blessing rather than a curse for Uganda.
Speaking at a Commonwealth heads of government conference on reform of international institutions such as the International Monetary Fund in London, Museveni said Uganda's main problem was selling its food to global markets.

Original_Search_Title: Calliandra Tree Boosts Honey Production
Headline: Uganda: Calliandra Tree Boosts Honey Production
Body_Text:
Kampala — A COMMON exotic tree species introduced in the country about two decades ago as fodder for animals, has been found to have high nectar content.
Bee researchers in Tororo District found out that the tree is capable of increasing Uganda's annual honey production.

Original_Search_Title: These Poor Kids
Headline: Uganda: These Poor Kids
Body_Text:
Kampala — After the jigger revelations of two weeks ago, some sadistic people have been waiting for me to drop some more historical skeletons. There was the time I stepped carelessly into the folds of my gomesi, and it unravelled - leaving me with nothing on but the big "belt". Thankfully there is no photographic evidence so I can forget about the trauma.
The more relevant trauma is that of today's children. While I am thankful that their return to school has decongested the city and, more importantly, made Garden City accessible, I can't help but feel sorry for them. Things now are so much harder than they were in the good old days.

Original_Search_Title: One Toilet for Kasekulo
Headline: Uganda: One Toilet for Kasekulo
Body_Text:
Kampala — Kasekulo landing site is inhabited by over 2,000 people who share one latrine. Godfrey Kimono visited the site and now writes
THE smell hits you from a distance of three kilometres away. The wooden houses come into view followed by the fishing nets and then the canoes at the waterfront. Soon you have reached the source of the smell: fish that has spread out on the sand to dry.

Original_Search_Title: Gov't to Carry Out Food Study
Headline: Rwanda: Gov't to Carry Out Food Study
Body_Text:
Kigali — The Ministry of Commerce is to carry out a study on all locally produced foodstuff, the Secretary General has revealed yesterday.
"We will be provided with all the information about food production and costs, which will help in coming up with a better assessment on how to fix prices of sensitive foodstuffs," said Antoine Ruvebana.

Original_Search_Title: Migraine - Glitz Save a Life With Eudora N Ndubisi
Headline: Nigeria: Migraine - Glitz Save a Life With Eudora N Ndubisi
Body_Text:
Lagos — Migraines affect women 3 times more than they affect men. A migraine headache is often disabling with associated symptoms such as flashes of light, visual blind spots and tingling in the arms or legs.
In some cases, these disabling headaches are accompanied or preceded with an "Aura" which is a sensory warning signal. Other possible symptoms include nausea and vomiting, extreme sensitivity to light (photophobia) and extreme sensitivity to sound. The pain of a migraine headache could be excruciating enough to incapacitate a sufferer for hours or even days.

Original_Search_Title: Global Sales of Fairtrade Coffee, Flowers Improve
Headline: Africa: Global Sales of Fairtrade Coffee, Flowers Improve
Body_Text:
Global sales of fairtrade products jumped by 47 per cent last year, as ethically produced flowers, coffee and other goods reached new markets.
Consumers spent more than 2.3 billion on fairtrade goods during 2007, shows a new report from the fairtrade Labelling Organisation (FLO), with premiums earned on the products going to more than 1.5 million producers and workers in 58 countries.

Original_Search_Title: AC Raps Iwu Over Defence of 2007 Polls
Headline: Nigeria: AC Raps Iwu Over Defence of 2007 Polls
Body_Text:
Lagos — The Action Congress (AC) has described as despicable, offensive and a reflection of warped thinking the continuous defence of the 2007 general elections by INEC chairman Maurice Iwu, even when the polls have been widely adjudged as the worst in the nation's history.
National Publicity Secretary of the party, Alhaji Lai Mohammed, in a statement yesterday also expressed total shock and outrage at Iwu's denigration of the media, the international observers, Nigerians and opposition politicians in a newspaper interview he granted recently.

Original_Search_Title: Local Products Excel At UMA Western Tradeshow
Headline: Uganda: Local Products Excel At UMA Western Tradeshow
Body_Text:
Kampala — Local exhibitors at the Uganda Manufacturers Association regional exhibition in Mbarara left show goers satisfied with locally-produced products.
The move to have Mbarara host the exhibition is an attempt to reach the wider market outside Kampala and also dispel the notion that all successful exhibitions must be in the capital city-Kampala.

Original_Search_Title: Three SA 'Newsmen' Jailed
Headline: Zimbabwe: Three SA 'Newsmen' Jailed
Body_Text:
Harare — THREE South Africans who were intercepted at a police roadblock in Mbalabala last Thursday and found in possession of broadcasting equipment bearing logos of a British television station, Sky News, were yesterday each sentenced to an effective six months in prison.
Bernet Hasani Sono (34), Resemate Boy Chauke (46) and Simon Maodi, alias Musimani (38), pleaded guilty to contravening Section 33 (1) of the Postal and Telecommunications Act, Chapter 12.03, before Matabeleland provincial magistrate Mr John Masimba.

Original_Search_Title: Those Who Pay for Beer But Want Free Water Deserve to Die
Headline: Uganda: Those Who Pay for Beer But Want Free Water Deserve to Die
Body_Text:
On the weekend I watched the recent Pearl of Africa rally on TV. The rally's outcome was remarkably symbolic of the fate of Uganda. No matter how many times you see it, you never cease to be amazed by how green most of the Uganda countryside is.
Green, that is, compared to many countries in Africa. Yet, looking at the lush countryside, the weather-beaten barefoot villagers jumping about in uncontrollable excitement as the cars zoomed past seemed out of place, if not a total contradiction. You would have expected them to look as prosperous as the land was green.

Original_Search_Title: Father, 4 Sons Held Over Murder
Headline: Uganda: Father, 4 Sons Held Over Murder
Body_Text:
Kampala — A father and his four sons, including a 15-year-old, were among the six suspects arrested over a murder in Masaka. They allegedly killed Geofrey Kazoola, a Lukaya-based bodaboda cyclist, on May 20 and fled with his motorcycle.
The southern regional Police commander, Andrew Sorowen, identified the suspects as Hajji Ahmad Ssekyewa, 58, and his sons Mohammed Luboowa, 20, Bashir Mwanje, 19 and the two others aged 17 and 15.

Original_Search_Title: Local Fruit Firm Chosen for Global Award
Headline: Uganda: Local Fruit Firm Chosen for Global Award
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDA'S Fruits of the Nile (FON) renewable energy-promoting firm has been selected for the 2008 global green energy final contest where the winner will get £40,000 (about sh130m).
The company, which operates in the southern Uganda town of Njeru, uses the sun to dry fruits for export.

Original_Search_Title: Churning Money From Banana Leaves
Headline: Uganda: Churning Money From Banana Leaves
Body_Text:
Mbarara — Traditionally, dry banana leaves are used for mulching and making makeshift beds for mourners at burial sites; But Ms Abia Nuwabaine, Ms Mary Kobusingye and others have a different idea; they churn money out of them.
Ms Nuwabaine and her friends are using a combination of dry leaves and scrap paper to make organic beads from which they make necklaces, bangles, earrings and other accessories.

Original_Search_Title: In Memory of Nkuringo the Gorilla Which Protected Others
Headline: Uganda: In Memory of Nkuringo the Gorilla Which Protected Others
Body_Text:
Kampala — NKURINGO was a gorilla in the twilight of his life. At 50, Nkuringo was wounded by old age. He was a symbol of strength that helped him become the king of the 17 gorillas. At the time of his death, he walked lazily, had lost most of his teeth and ate less often.
James Byamukama of the International Gorilla Conservation Programme (IGCP) says: "Even the little food that was getting into the stomach was being eaten by parasites."

Original_Search_Title: Japan Finds New Textile Options
Headline: Uganda: Japan Finds New Textile Options
Body_Text:
Kampala — A Japanese professor and her students, whose research led to the production of textile materials from banana fibre, will visit Uganda soon. President Yoweri Museveni invited Prof. Kyoko Hashimoto on Thursday when he met her at the Intercontinental Hotel.
Museveni, who has been in Japan for the fourth Tokyo International Conference on African Development, was impressed by the materials, which included two shirts, one in the colours of Uganda's flag.

Original_Search_Title: Bigodi Swamp Uproots Poverty
Headline: Uganda: Bigodi Swamp Uproots Poverty
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE swamp was always there. Measuring about 4sq km, the swamp was a popular hunting ground.
That was before 1992. Fast forward - 2008 - the swamp is still there, but this time, it is uprooting poverty from the village. Because of the swamp, Bigodi village, 39km from Fort Portal on the road to Kamwenge, is one of the few villages in the country where most people live above the poverty line, thanks to a small swamp known as Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary.

Original_Search_Title: The Senate - Tranquility As Mark's Trophy
Headline: Nigeria: The Senate - Tranquility As Mark's Trophy
Body_Text:
Lagos — The low expectations that shadowed the advent of the sixth Senate may not have been totally dusted. But one cleaning up that has been achieved by the David Mark Senate is the unusual tranquility in the otherwise restive Senate of the fourth republic.It was unheard of in the fourth republic that a Senate President would have a daily devotion to playing golf.
Many of the presiding officers that preceded the incumbent rather than the daily visit to the golf course spent their time out of chamber in plotting one intrigue or the other to cage the opposition.

Original_Search_Title: Country Has No Business Hosting World Cup
Headline: South Africa: Coffee Break is Over
Body_Text:
We have always known South African black people to be even more discriminatory than the whites who rode roughshod over them during apartheid. We just didn't think they'd go this far- beating and killing fellow blacks from other countries, blaming them for their economic woes.
You are tempted to think the calm they have enjoyed between 1990-2008 was simply a coffee break which is now over, and normal service has resumed: discrimination and violence.

Original_Search_Title: Namboole, a Magnet of Action
Headline: Uganda: Namboole, a Magnet of Action
Body_Text:
The big game is on tomorrow as Uganda Cranes takes on Niger in a Nations Cup qualifier at Namboole stadium. However, there is more to this sports complex than big games and crusades, especially just outside the complex.
The neat and smartly tarmacked ground on the Western end that faces Kampala, with patches of shoe-sole-levelled green, serves as a packing lot during functions at the stadium but also serves as a leisure and recreation park of sorts at other times.

Original_Search_Title: Global Green Awards - 2008 Finalists Announced
Headline: Africa: Global Green Awards - 2008 Finalists Announced
Body_Text:
Today the world's leading green energy prize announced that pioneering renewable energy projects from Africa, Asia and Latin America will each receive prizes of up to £20,000, with an overall Energy Champion winning up to £40,000. Winners will be revealed at a ceremony in London in June.
World wide, two billion people still have no access to modern forms of energy. 1.6 million women and children are dying every year from respiratory diseases because they are cooking over open fires. The finalists' efforts highlight how simple design innovation, commitment, and relatively small financial outlay can bring huge benefits to large numbers of people in terms of health, education, social welfare, and finance. The projects recognised and rewarded this year – which include small and large scale solar, mini hydro power schemes, and fuel-efficient stoves running off waste products – transform lives and reduce poverty in their communities.

Original_Search_Title: Women Left for Dead - And the Man Who's Saving Them
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Women Left for Dead - And the Man Who's Saving Them
Body_Text:
In the Congo, where tens of thousands of women are brutally raped every year, Dr. Denis Mukwege repairs their broken bodies and souls. Eve Ensler visits him and finds hope amid the horror.
I have just returned from hell. I am trying for the life of me to figure out how to communicate what I have seen and heard in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. How do I convey these stories of atrocities without your shutting down, quickly turning the page or feeling too disturbed?

Original_Search_Title: Clobbering 'Amakwerekwere'
Headline: South Africa: Clobbering 'Amakwerekwere'
Body_Text:
Amakwerekwere. This highly pejorative term is what a good number of black South Africans call those black people from the rest of Africa who have come streaming into their country since the end of Apartheid in 1994 and stayed on, the vast majority of them illegally.
These poor foreigners, whom we see equally poor South Africans clobbering in Interahamwe-like violence on TV, are just a few of the millions of illegal immigrants living in the country's big cities.

Original_Search_Title: 'Democracy is Not a Privilege'
Headline: Zimbabwe: 'Democracy is Not a Privilege'
Body_Text:
Washington, DC — "Africa waged a century-long struggle against colonialism and apartheid precisely to establish the principle that governments should derive legitimacy through the consent of the governed.
Democratic institutions are therefore not privileges that may be extended or withheld at the discretion of those who wield power." - Pallo Jordan

Original_Search_Title: Schools Should Not Hike Fees
Headline: Uganda: Schools Should Not Hike Fees
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRIMARY and secondary schools across the country reopen today for the new academic terms. At the end of the first term, last month, many schools, particularly with boarding facilities, announced increase of their charges, citing the rise in the prices of basic commodities like maize flour, beef, banana, chicken and fuel.
At the Ndejje University, the increase in the charges sparked off violent protest by the students forcing the administration to close the university.

Original_Search_Title: The Attack On Mengo, the Kabaka's Escape
Headline: Uganda: The Attack On Mengo, the Kabaka's Escape
Body_Text:
Kampala — It was bound to happen. Following Mengo's resolution to evict the central government from its land, Uganda Prime Minister Dr. Apollo Milton Obote ordered Government troops to attack the Kabaka's palace at Mengo. In his own words, Kabaka Mutesa recalls the battle to defend his throne.
COUNTDOWN

Original_Search_Title: Migrants Face Tough Choices
Headline: South Africa: Should I Stay Or Should I Go?
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — Downtown Johannesburg was as busy as ever on Thursday afternoon. Gospel music blared from speakers, commuters and school children wove their way through the jammed market stalls and food outlets on Noord Street before boarding the taxis crawling bumper-to-bumper up Twist Street.
On Monday it had been different. A rumour that the "foreigners", who have their stalls in the area, were next to be attacked in the xenophobic violence that has flared across South Africa's largest city since 10 May, emptied Noord Street as the Nigerian, Zimbabwean and Mozambican traders closed shop.

Original_Search_Title: Redrawing Our Political Map
Headline: Nigeria: Redrawing Our Political Map
Body_Text:
Lagos — Our nation's Constitution accepts the fact that since our representatives at the various Houses of Assembly are chosen by the people it means that as nearly as practicable, one man's vote in any of the elections is of the same worth and value with the vote of any other man.
Essentially, it gives full credence to the much paraded phrase, "One man, one vote". Justice William Brennan expresses this view most succinctly: "Equal representation for equal numbers of people is a principle designed to prevent debasement of voting power and diminution of access to elected Representatives. Toleration of even small deviations detracts from these purposes".

Original_Search_Title: Man Held Over Hacking 'Lover'
Headline: Uganda: Man Held Over Hacking 'Lover'
Body_Text:
Kampala — A MAN who allegedly hacked his 24-year-old girlfriend, accusing her of having an affair with another man, has been arrested.
Robert Mubangizi was last week detained at Ntungamo Police Station for reportedly injuring Allen Ninsiima, who is admitted at Mbarara referral hospital with machete wounds on the face.

Original_Search_Title: Democracy is Not a Privilege
Headline: South Africa: Democracy is Not a Privilege
Body_Text:
Speaking in parliament during the budget debate of the Minister of Foreign Affairs in 2003, amongst other things I said:
"Like peace and stability, democracy and good governance are developmental issues. Africa waged a century-long struggle against colonialism and apartheid precisely to establish the principle that governments should derive legitimacy through the consent of the governed. Democratic institutions are therefore not privileges that may be extended or withheld at the discretion of those who wield power. They are an entitlement; a right that the people of this continent waged struggle to attain and won at great cost!

Original_Search_Title: Restaurant Meal Costs Zim $2.5 Billion
Headline: Zimbabwe: Dusty Miller - Currying Flavour!
Body_Text:
THERE was no question of eating out for less than $1 billion at Sitar, Newlands, on Monday, after working rather late.
Monday isn't great for dining out as many standalone restaurants shut, having worked what is now perceived in the hospitality industry as unsocial hours over the weekend.

Original_Search_Title: Food Crisis - Jailed Suspects Starve
Headline: Zimbabwe: Food Crisis - Jailed Suspects Starve
Body_Text:
Harare — ZIMBABWE'S over-crowded jails have run out of food and prison officials are asking relatives to bring supplementary supplies for inmates, The Standard has been told.
Sources said this week most prisons had food supplies for less than a month. They said the situation was most desperate at Harare remand prison where the conditions for visiting suspects have been relaxed to allow relatives to bring additional food for inmates.

Original_Search_Title: Deal On the Basis of Mutual Gain
Headline: Uganda: Deal On the Basis of Mutual Gain
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni was recently in Israel on a state visit where he addressed an international conference called by the Israeli President Shimon Peres.
YOUR Excellencies, I greet you all. On behalf of the people of the Republic of Uganda, I salute Your Excellency, President Peres, for the long and distinguished service to your country - Israel.

Original_Search_Title: Power Probe - Some Comments On Obasanjo's Letter
Headline: Nigeria: Power Probe - Some Comments On Obasanjo's Letter
Body_Text:
Abuja — There appear to be two main ways to react, and to weigh the worth of the House of Representatives Committee on Power and Steel's current probe of the power projects scandal. It is either you enjoy the show for its value, or doubt the integrity of its substance.
But first, the process is refreshing, revealing, and perhaps, reassuring. To elaborate, it is refreshing in that some former powerful persons who, not too long ago, acted as if Nigeria had been sold to them, have been compelled by the forces of public demand and justice, to take the stand, right there, in the well of the National Assembly chambers, to answer to some pointed questions about their stewardship. It is also revealing about what magnitude of rot can be generated in any administration that lacks honest, moral leadership, sincerity and respect for public sensibilities. The committee's work is equally reassuring that Nigeria will work, though we may not know when. Indeed, we can even begin to fantasise with the scenario that some Nigerians may someday emerge, to undertake the task of verifying what could have bedevilled the country for so long. This, for now, is the highest point in the general therapeutic effects of what Mr. Ndudi Godwin Elumelu and other compatriots are trying to do in the House.

Original_Search_Title: Procurement Price Lists Faulted
Headline: Uganda: Procurement Price Lists Faulted
Body_Text:
Kampala — In a bid to fight procurement corruption in public offices, the Ugandan Government has issued prices for common consumer goods that are clearly at odds with the surging market rates.
At the end of 2007, the Public Procurement and Disposal of Public Assets Authority (PPDA) compiled a 74-page list of common user items and average prices for suppliers and services, which it has now issued to accounting officers and those involved with tendering and purchases.

Original_Search_Title: Consumption of Alcohol By Youth Alarming
Headline: Uganda: Consumption of Alcohol By Youth Alarming
Body_Text:
Kampala — LIKE any other student on vacation, 19-year-old Fred Sakwa desperately wanted a part time job to keep him busy.
He had completed his S6 and it would be a long time before university admissions begin. Luckily, he got a job as a sales attendant at a telecom shop on Luwum Street in Kampala. At the end of each month, he smiled all the way to the bank.

Original_Search_Title: The Looming Anarchy in South West
Headline: Nigeria: The Looming Anarchy in South West
Body_Text:
I do not think anybody needs a prophet to know that a complete breakdown of law and order is imminent in the South West region of the country where recent events indicate a reversion to the stone-age where rulers instigate violence and mayhem on perceived political enemies.
The spiraling violence and crass lawlessness in three South-west states of and Oyo,Osun and Ekiti states have made a state of anarchy very likely.Only Sunday, two policemen were killed in continuation of the spate of political violence that has been ravaging Osun State since the massively rigged April general elections. The officers were murdered by unidentified gunmen in the course of trying to unravel the circumstances surrounding recent bomb explosions in the state.

Original_Search_Title: European Union Sues for Assets Declaration, Credible Elecam
Headline: Cameroon: European Union Sues for Assets Declaration, Credible Elecam
Body_Text:
The European Union, EU, has recommended that for the fight against corruption to be effective, politicians should declare their assets.
It also sued for the credibility of officials to be appointed to head Elections Cameroon, ELECAM.The Ambassador and Chief of European Commission Delegation to Cameroon, Janvier Puyol, made the recommendation at a dinner on May 9, on the occasion of the European Day in Yaounde.

Original_Search_Title: Beach Paradise From the Inside Out
Headline: Kenya: Beach Paradise From the Inside Out
Body_Text:
Nairobi — TOURIST PARADISES ARE not quite the same when you live in them rather than stay for a few days and return to the Big Smoke.
You don't notice it when you're put up in a hotel, but in this part of the world, the process of rotting and ripening is greatly hastened. Nothing lasts for long and decay is swift and unrelenting.

Original_Search_Title: Incredibly, Our Ministers Have a Conscience
Headline: Uganda: Incredibly, Our Ministers Have a Conscience
Body_Text:
Kampala — Uganda sometimes surprises. About two weeks ago, our government ministers hinted that they may after all have something similar to a conscience.
No grand display; no exhibition of good works by the ministers in the name of some high human cause. Traces of the said conscience were detected because members of the group showed that they would generally prefer to hide their greed (see "We are broke, ministers cry"; Daily Monitor, April 29).

Original_Search_Title: Deport Italian, Says Kajwang'
Headline: Kenya: Deport Italian, Says Kajwang'
Body_Text:
Nairobi — An Italian who has been living in Kenya illegally for the last 18 years will be arrested and deported.
Immigration minister, Mr Otieno Kajwang', ordered Mr Canobbio Pietro's deportation.

Original_Search_Title: Nnabagereka Tips Women On Land
Headline: Uganda: Nnabagereka Tips Women On Land
Body_Text:
Kampala — WOMEN should exploit the abundant land in their communities for development instead of depending on their husbands, the Nnabagereka (queen) of Buganda Kingdom has counselled.
Sylvia Nagginda added that women should also not wait for the Government to provide them with everything.

Original_Search_Title: Jittery Markets Worry Domestic Banks - Study
Headline: South Africa: Jittery Markets Worry Domestic Banks - Study
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — SOUTH African banks are nervous about credit and market-related risks, and the effect of a volatile currency on their business, according to the latest survey of potential risks global banks are worried about.
The Banking Banana Skins report for this year, to be published today, said banks in SA were also worried about access to foreign markets for funding as anxiety levels in the global financial markets have shot up to a 10-year high.

Original_Search_Title: Farmers Reap Benefits of Fair Trade Teas
Headline: Kenya: Farmers Reap Benefits of Fair Trade Teas
Body_Text:
Boosted by increased demand for fair trade certified products in international markets, Kenya's tea sector is now turning to the fair trade movement to increase revenue.
In the last one year, seven tea factories allied to the Kenya Tea Development Authority (KTDA) have entered the certification programme.

Original_Search_Title: Briquettes - Building Blocks for a Clean Environment
Headline: Rwanda: Briquettes - Building Blocks for a Clean Environment
Body_Text:
When you cook, you create garbage. Now an association in Nyakabanda has found a way to use garbage for cooking. "At first, it was a bit hard to convince people to pay us for collecting their garbage, but as soon as they saw that it kept the neighborhood clean, they easily agreed."
Beata Uwimpuhwe is the president of the Association pour la Conservation de l'Enviroment (Association for environmental conservation, ACEN), which in 2002 took the bold step to ask money from the residents of Nyakabanda and Kimisagara to collect their waste, something which was until then taken care of by the districts.

Original_Search_Title: Women Protest Against Militants
Headline: Nigeria: Women Protest Against Militants
Body_Text:
Hundreds of women in Bayelsa State embarked on a protest rally weekend against perceived engagement of youths and women in political violence in the Niger Delta states, calling on politicians to desist from the act and give peace a chance.
The women, under the aegis of the Bayelsa Women for Peace, were in their hundreds and led by the state chairperson of the National Council for Women Societies, Mrs. Abosede Apere.

Original_Search_Title: Govt Won't Fix Food Prices, Says Finance Minister
Headline: Uganda: Govt Won't Fix Prices
Body_Text:
Kampala — As prices of food and other commodities continue to sky-rocket, Finance Minister Ezra Suruma has told members of Parliament that the government will not fix prices.
"We cannot fix commodity prices because doing so is a hopeless way of managing the economy," Dr Suruma told the Parliamentary Finance Committee on Thursday. "For the government to come in and stop these soaring prices distorts the economy yet we don't want to go back to where we were many years ago."

Original_Search_Title: Food Prices Worry Bushenyi Residents
Headline: Uganda: Food Prices Worry Bushenyi Residents
Body_Text:
Kampala — RESIDENTS of Kyamuhunga sub-county in Bushenyi district on Thursday put to task ministers Tarsis Kabwegyere (disaster preparedness) and Richard Nduhura (state health) to explain why the prices of foodstuffs were not stable.
The LC1 chairman of Ryakatakanya village, Phenecas Barigye, said residents were worried about the rate at which the prices of salt and soap were increasing. This was during the Labour Day celebrations at Kyamuhunga SS on Thursday.

Original_Search_Title: Banker Hopes to Rekindle JM Kariuki's Dream
Headline: Kenya: Banker Hopes to Rekindle JM Kariuki's Dream
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Seven years ago, he had no intentions of vying for an elective office.
When he left his lofty perch in Nairobi, the banker suffered as he tried to adjust to rural life. This was after staying in the city for 31 years.

Original_Search_Title: Militia Drive Opposition Supporters From Homes
Headline: Zimbabwe: Corpses Rot As Zanu PF Terror Moves a Gear Up
Body_Text:
Harare — At least four bodies of MDC supporters, reportedly murdered in the on-going political violence, are decomposing at Guruve Hospital in Mashonaland Central after their relatives fled their homes to seek refuge in urban areas.
Normally the relatives would have taken care of their burial arrangements.

Original_Search_Title: Illegal Fishing Costs U.S. $1 Billion Per Year
Headline: Africa: Illegal Fishing Costs Continent Sh62 Billion
Body_Text:
London — The scale of illegal fishing across Africa is now so serious that it is in danger of decimating stocks across the continent, a new report says.
The report represents the first detailed quantitative analysis of the problem on a global scale and studies indicate that losses for sub-Saharan Africa total $1 billion per year.

Original_Search_Title: Good to Be in the EAC
Headline: East Africa: Good to Be in the EAC
Body_Text:
Kigali — The last couple of years have, sadly, made me the archetypal Rwandan 'muturage'. I've been cocooned in my little world that was composed of Kigali, Butare and a couple other environs of the picturesque land of 'a thousand hills'.
I've been in this country so long that I'd forgotten that there was a world out there that didn't involve the things I was familiar with. Sometimes I think that some of the challenges that we face as a nation are a result of this self-imposed isolation.

Original_Search_Title: Unilever Goes for Value Addition in New Profit Drive
Headline: Kenya: Unilever Goes for Value Addition in New Profit Drive
Body_Text:
Unilever Tea Kenya Limited, is banking on value added products and certified teas to recover from a Sh110 million management and staff restructuring charge that ate into its profits last year.
The firm's books of account for the year ended December 2007 show it posted a loss of Sh104 million. The management has attributed this to a fluctuating local currency, high cost of wages and the restructuring that saw 25 per cent of its staff laid off.

Original_Search_Title: Lampshades to Reflect Rich African Culture
Headline: Kenya: Lampshades to Reflect Rich African Culture
Body_Text:
Three years ago, Terry Waweru of Terry Designs & Interior Decor started making African- inspired lampshades following demand . "My customers kept asking for them," she says, pointing at some samples lining one of the walls in her shop at the Adams Arcade shopping centre in Nairobi.
Her main business is curtains, seats and furnishings. Since her clients did not want to go out looking for lampshades, she decided to include them as an extra service. She soon got suppliers for the stands.

Original_Search_Title: Banks Set to Compete in Borderless World
Headline: Nigeria: Banks Set to Compete in Borderless World
Body_Text:
Lagos — Sean Moroney, Chairman of Aitec Africa has commended Nigeria for the dramatic transformation of its financial sector, which he described as probably unprecedented in its scale and impact, compared with anywhere in the world.
Moroney made the commendation while unveiling the theme for the forthcoming African Banking Technology Conference which is scheduled to hold from May 6-7 in Lagos.

Original_Search_Title: Etisalat Gears Up for Commercial Launch
Headline: Nigeria: Etisalat Gears Up for Commercial Launch
Body_Text:
To further demonstrate its preparedness to offer Nigerians world class telecommunications services and it's proposed commercial launch in no distant time, Etisalat, Nigeria's fifth licensed GSM operator has relocated to its own newly acquired corporate office complex.
The property located in the highbrow Banana Island, Ikoyi, Lagos provides Etisalat top rate employees with the right environment to function optimally. "Acquiring our own property lends credence to the fact that Etisalat believes in Nigeria and has indeed come to stay.

Original_Search_Title: Commodity Prices Soar
Headline: Uganda: Commodity Prices Soar
Body_Text:
Kampala — As city dwellers grapple with soaring commodity prices, analysts are predicting that the urban poor will be even under greater strain if regional markets remain competitive.
As traders find comparatively high prices for their produce in foreign markets, consumers in Kampala must dig deeper into their pockets, the analysts say.

Original_Search_Title: Family Planning a Matter of Urgency, Says Minister
Headline: Rwanda: Family Planning a Matter of Urgency, Says Minister
Body_Text:
Rubavu — The Minister of Health, Dr. Jean-Damascène Ntawukuriryayo, has called for immediate action to assist Government in its efforts to reduce the country's alarming population growth rate.
Currently estimated to be about 10 million, Rwanda's population growth rate is among the highest in the region.

Original_Search_Title: Etisalat Sets for Commercial Launch
Headline: Nigeria: Etisalat Sets for Commercial Launch
Body_Text:
Lagos — Etisalat, Nigeria's fifth licensed GSM operator has relocated to its own newly acquired corporate office complex, a statement from the company has said.
The relocation, the statement said, is to further demonstrate the company's preparedness to offer Nigerians world class telecommunications services.

Original_Search_Title: 'Banking Technology Conference Looks At Borderless World'
Headline: Africa: 'Banking Technology Conference Looks At Borderless World'
Body_Text:
The theme for the forthcoming African Banking Technology Conference which is scheduled to hold from May 6-7 next month at the Eko Hotel, Victoria Island, Lagos, is: 'Competing in a borderless world.'
Sean Moroney, chairman of Aitec Africa, the oragnisers of the conference, while announcing plans for the third African Banking Technology Conference in Lagos recently, said Nigeria is engaged in a dramatic transformation of its financial sector, "probably unprecedented in its scale and impact compared with anywhere in the world."

Original_Search_Title: Hanging in the Art Gallery
Headline: Uganda: Hanging in the Art Gallery
Body_Text:
Kampala — Why don't Africans see the value in African Art galleries? Moses Opopo walked around town to see what our houses of art have to offer the typical Ugandan...
C hantal Berthomiea is the first client to disrupt the unusually quiet peace at Tulifanya Gallery And Coffee House behind Crested Towers on this Wednesday morning. She heads straight to the exhibition hall, where the contemporary art pieces of two local artists, Navio Barigye and Ronnie Ogwang are on display. Within a space of less than 10 minutes, she is paying sh400,000 for two acrylic wall pieces by Navio. Moving into the gift shop, which is stocked with an assortment of crafts and antiques, she picks up a miniature wood carving of a mask, bringing her collection to three. Chantal lives in Switzerland (she is French), and has been in Uganda for a week now. She still has one more week to stay in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Continent is Angry, Hungry, Violent
Headline: Africa: Continent is Angry, Hungry, Violent
Body_Text:
Kampala — A Parliamentary caucus of the ruling National Resistance Movement party recently requested the Government to make deliberate pro-active actions to address the ever-increasing cost of food and consumer commodities. The caucus specifically requested the Government to waive Value Added Tax on food products. The concern expressed by the NRM MPs feeds into what is now viewed as a global food crisis. Although sections of the international media have blamed this crisis on bad governance in developing countries, food shortages have been reported in most parts of the world.
However, Africa's culture of bad governance and lack of strategic planning compounds food shortages. That is why there are fears that the now-incessant food shortages could lead to civil unrest; for is it not said that 'a hungry man is an angry man'? Indeed, the scenario of poor masses struggling with food costs (whether caused by global oil prices, weather and speculators trading in local capital markets), is not a good proposition for Africa's pseudo-democrats.

Original_Search_Title: Unctad Hears Gender Inequality Becoming Worse - And Better
Headline: Africa: Unctad Hears Gender Inequality Becoming Worse - And Better
Body_Text:
Accra — The only way that the poor, particularly women, will benefit from all the efforts that the United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD) has put into improving global trade is to ensure that power inequalities are redressed.
This is the comment of Esther Busser, trade policy advisor of the Geneva-based International Trade Union Confederation, who is attending the 12th UNCTAD meeting in Accra, Ghana. It started on April 20 and concludes tomorrow. The confederation is a non-governmental organisation (NGO) working towards realising workers' rights.

Original_Search_Title: Pray That Museveni Follows Kaunda's, Not Mugabe's Road
Headline: Uganda: Pray That Museveni Follows Kaunda's, Not Mugabe's Road
Body_Text:
Why, you ask, would someone like Zimbabwe's President Robert Mugabe make so much personal sacrifice, endure prison and horrific torture to lead a liberation war in his country then go on to inflict deeper wounds on it than the white supremacist settlers did?
Or for that matter, why would President Yoweri Museveni take to the bush partly in outrage after the UPC stole the December 1980 elections, go through a war in which thousands were killed, and then (as the Supreme Court found) himself rig two successive elections in 2001 and 2006?

Original_Search_Title: Spain Extending Its Reach Into Africa
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Spain Extending Its Reach Into Africa
Body_Text:
Seville — Spain has expanded its presence in Africa by recently signing trade agreements with Egypt and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). The agreement with Egypt includes regular bilateral summits similar to Spain's annual meetings with Morocco, Algeria and Tunisia. Both agreements will facilitate the entry of more Spanish companies into Africa.
In the agreement with Egypt, funds are made available for Egyptian companies to purchase Spanish products and services.

Original_Search_Title: This Deal Must Not Be Another Rip-Off
Headline: Kenya: This Deal Must Not Be Another Rip-Off
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Finally, the grand coalition has given birth to a grand Cabinet which it is hoped, will lead Kenyans back to the grand march they hesitantly started in 1992.
And even better, there is now talk of a grand opposition by the backbenchers in Parliament to provide the checks and balances necessary to ensure the grand alliance does not pull us back into the cesspool of grand corruption that has been the country's bane since the 1980s.

Original_Search_Title: More Officers Deployed to Quell Chaos
Headline: Kenya: More Officers Deployed to Quell Chaos
Body_Text:
Nairobi — More police officers have been deployed in Nairobi and its environs to suppress the Mungiki threat.
Sect members have been moving around estates in Nairobi, ordering residents not to go to work. The threats, according to police, are given verbally by sect members at bus stops.

Original_Search_Title: Victim Explains Resistance of Mwulire Insurgents During Genocide
Headline: Rwanda: Victim Explains Resistance of Mwulire Insurgents During Genocide
Body_Text:
Rwamagana — The victims of the 1994 genocide of the Mwulire sector in Rwamagana district, Eastern Province, waged a hard fought resistance to their attackers but succumbed to their strength and large numbers, Hirondelle Agency has learned.
"We held back for a quite a while the attacks of the Interahamwe militia by means of stones, bows and spears. Seeing that we were not giving up, they [attackers] called upon gendarmes and soldiers of the presidential guard. Many among us fell to the bullets from their guns in the attack... We are a handful of people to have survived ", said Francois Rutareka, a former counsellor in the sector when giving personal experience at the closing ceremony of the mourning week in Rwamagana district on Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: Without Goodwill, We Are Headed for Another Gridlock
Headline: Kenya: Without Goodwill, We Are Headed for Another Gridlock
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It is now clear that PNU and ODM differ fundamentally in their interpretations of the National Accord laws. And given the situation, we must also face up to the question of whether the "principals" can ever work together productively. The experience since 2003 has not been a happy one.
Even if a lean cabinet is realised and portfolios balanced properly and proportionately, I forsee continuing problems when it comes to the civil service bureaucracy. Start from the top with permanent secretaries. Even if by happenstance the ODM get to have its share of them, the chain of authority will still be blurred. As things stand, the president can conceivably fire them whenever he chooses (which he cannot do with ODM-appointed ministers). It is not clear Mr Raila Odinga can fire even those PSs whose appointments he would have influenced.

Original_Search_Title: What It Takes to Be a Good Leader
Headline: Kenya: What It Takes to Be a Good Leader
Body_Text:
Nairobi — One positive development from the row over the Cabinet is the emergence of the debate on the quality of political leadership Kenya deserves.
It would be a great development for Kenya if the debate continued so that the country may have comprehensive criteria for determining whether or not actual and potential leaders meet them.

Original_Search_Title: Our Micro-Economic Climate Suffocates Growth
Headline: Namibia: Our Micro-Economic Climate Suffocates Growth
Body_Text:
Windhoek — How do Namibian firms benefit from budget allocations? I'm raising these issues, because in my view, Namibian companies are being crowded out of the Namibian market by foreign companies who are awash with money, and some of them which are alleged to be enjoying some subsidies from their parent companies and governments of their country of origin.
And while I'm on this point, let me state that I'm not advancing this point from an influence of xenophobia or protectionism, but I'm voicing the concern shared by many struggling Namibian small and medium business men and women, and many workers of Namibia who are feeling the pinch - who are being suffocated by the domination of the Namibian market by foreign multi-nationals and other foreign firms which are taking bread from their mouths, and pushing them (Namibian firms) out of the market.

Original_Search_Title: When the Going Gets Tough
Headline: Uganda: When the Going Gets Tough
Body_Text:
Kampala — When the going gets tough, the tough get? Well, it really depends on whom you ask.
MTN Heathens will hasten to tell you that the tough get going, citing their 39-3 win over Entebbe Mongers last weekend. In truth, the duel was supposed to be as lopsided as they get.

Original_Search_Title: Trade Experts Seek Greater Information Sharing Among ACP Countries
Headline: Africa: Trade Experts Seek Greater Information Sharing Among ACP Countries
Body_Text:
Participants call on Commonwealth, ACP to support sensitisation of officials on new trading regimes
Trade experts from Africa, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) have called for greater information sharing among the member states to ensure that there is better understanding of the lessons learnt in negotiating new trade arrangements with the European Union, known as Economic Partnership Agreements (EPAs).

Original_Search_Title: Adding Value to Matooke Will Increase Farmers' Incomes
Headline: Uganda: Adding Value to Matooke Will Increase Farmers' Incomes
Body_Text:
Kampala — In recent years, value addition to matooke, to make it more valuable and exportable has been carried out and will soon spread throughout all matooke-growing areas of Uganda.
Matooke can now be made into flour that can be eaten as porridge or meal. The flour can be used to make cakes, bread, biscuits, doughnuts and chapatis. You can also make chips from matooke.

Original_Search_Title: The Jewel of Butare
Headline: Rwanda: The Jewel of Butare
Body_Text:
Kigali — Half an hour is a very selfish way of delving into the entire rich and diverse history of not only a nation but a unique group of people.
Yet this is exactly the time I had to dive into the long imposing brick and metal structure just before Butare town, which in itself looks a well conserved relic of history, which houses the national museum of Rwanda.

Original_Search_Title: Thumbs Up As David Marks 60
Headline: Nigeria: Thumbs Up As David Marks 60
Body_Text:
Lagos — In this tribute to the Senate President, Senator David Alechenu Bonaventure Mark, on his 60th birthday that comes up today, April 8, 2008, Sufuyan Ojeifo profiles the man's past achievements, his politics and leadership of the Senate
It is celebration time with many 'gun' salutes for David Alechenu Bonaventure Mark. And, this is understandable. He is marking his 60th year on earth on Tuesday, April 8 and he is doing it in style: on the saddle as President of the Nigerian Senate. This is a mark of providence for the retired Brigadier-General of the Nigerian Army, an officer and a gentleman.

Original_Search_Title: Dakar On Less Than U.S. $5 a Day
Headline: Senegal: Dakar On Less Than U.S. $5 a Day
Body_Text:
Dakar — While driving a rusty old yellow taxi through Dakar's potholed streets looking for customers, Abdou Ndoye told IRIN how he and his family of nine gets by on the 2,000 CFA francs [US$4.80] he makes a day.
"Some times we eat more, other times less," he shouted above the noise of the car's rattling engine. "But we never go hungry. We make the money go far."

Original_Search_Title: Use of Biomass Fuel Hurting People's Health
Headline: Uganda: Use of Biomass Fuel Hurting People's Health
Body_Text:
Uganda is losing many opportunities for not passing and implementing the now dormant Draft National Biomass Energy Demand Strategy 2001-2010.
This draft strategy was finalised in 2000 to address the use and conservation of biomass resources. This strategy is vital for it touches on the biomass energy sector on which over 90% of Ugandans depend for their daily cooking needs. It targets 90,000 households countrywide to access biomass improved technologies so as to improve biomass efficiency to fight poverty, disease and environmental degradation.

Original_Search_Title: Kiambaa Still Haunted By Dark Past
Headline: Kenya: Kiambaa Still Haunted By Dark Past
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There were many atrocities committed during the post-election period. One of the most shocking was at the Kiambaa Assemblies of God Church near Eldoret where women and children, 35 in all, were burnt alive.
The fact that churches are universally considered the most sacrosanct places of refuge in times of conflict did not spare this particular church. Nor that there were mainly women and children hiding inside it.

Original_Search_Title: Our Micro-Economic Climate Suffocates Growth
Headline: Namibia: Our Micro-Economic Climate Suffocates Growth
Body_Text:
Windhoek — This paper dissects factors surrounding the Appropriation Bill for the financial years under consideration in the National Assembly at the moment.
The author welcomes the introduction of a micro-lending scheme as provided for in the current Appropriation Bill. The issue of a micro-lending scheme is very critical for assisting small and medium firms that play a pivotal role in the second phase of our liberation struggle, which is the reconstruction of the Namibian economy to benefit the majority of the Namibian people, especially the rural and urban poor.

Original_Search_Title: Implement National Biomass Energy Strategy
Headline: Uganda: Implement National Biomass Energy Strategy
Body_Text:
Consensus is growing on the fact that there is a direct relationship between Acute Respiratory Infections (ARIs) and biomass energy utilisation among rural households. For instance in 1997, the World Health Organisation (WHO) concluded that the greatest global burden of air pollution exposure occurs not outdoors in the cities of the developed world, but indoors in poor rural communities of many developing countries especially in Africa.
In 1999, it was estimated that ARIs accounted for 1.2 per cent of the burden of ill-health in sub-Saharan Africa compared to 0.8 per cent for malaria and 1.3 per cent for diarrhea (WHO, Geneva, 1999). In its 2001 report, WHO concluded that ARIs accounted for up to 6.5 per cent of the entire global burden of disease (WHO, 2001 ) The World Bank report on poverty and disease (2003) indicates that most of these social, economic and environmental problems more than any other, affect the poor of the poorest in poor countries. The report notes that more than half of the households around the world use biomass to meet their daily energy needs and that 90 per cent of these households are in Africa. Over the last decade, scholars have concluded that biomass use and associated exposure to indoor air pollution is a major cause of ARI especially among children less than five years of age.

Original_Search_Title: A Banana Republic in All But Name
Headline: Kenya: A Banana Republic in All But Name
Body_Text:
I CAN SURELY UNDERSTAND THE frustration felt by columnist Macharia Gaitho over delays in naming the Cabinet, but Kenyans should not "settle for a bloated Cabinet if that is what, alongside portfolio balance, will unlock the stalemate". Our politicians can, and must, do better!
Just recently, MPs approved a motion to raise their basic pay and allowances to more than Sh850,000 a month, while there has been little attempt to raise the salaries of other civil servants.

Original_Search_Title: Race of Shame
Headline: Cameroon: Race of Shame
Body_Text:
The ongoing cycling tour of Cameroon has been a succession of set backs, most of which can be blamed on poor organisation. It all began with the postponement of the start of the event by 24 hours due to delays in transporting members of the foreign delegation from Yaounde and Douala to Maroua.
Then came complains about poor feeding and lodging. To this has been added the late arrival of the bicycles of the riders from the Adamawa to the West Province and the reduction, by half, of the distance that had to be covered in stage 4. What a mess!

Original_Search_Title: Spiritual Leader Bemoans Fate of Post-Strike Detainees
Headline: Cameroon: Spiritual Leader Bemoans Fate of Post-Strike Detainees
Body_Text:
The leader of the Yaounde spiritual hospital, Bureau Lumiere, Marie Lumiere, has expressed deep concern about the arrest and detention of youths by the police and the gendarmerie during the February strike action.
"Many of these youths who are being prosecuted and jailed are as innocent as Jesus who suffered and was nailed on the cross for no crime," Marie Lumiere told a large crowd that turned out at the Mbankolo prayer ground recently.

Original_Search_Title: Murtala Instituted Corruption, Obasanjo Perfected It, Declares Rowland Owie
Headline: Nigeria: Murtala Instituted Corruption, Obasanjo Perfected It, Declares Rowland Owie
Body_Text:
Lagos — SENATOR Steven Rowland Owie is the leader of the Action Congress, AC, in Edo State. While the party is still partying endlessly in the state because of the victory at the Election Petition Tribunal, which gave victory to its governorship candidate, Comrade Adams Oshiomohle, Vanguard Politics met him for this interview and he expressed his feelings about the judgment, which he described as God's judgment and the best so far in the history of the nation.
As usual, the AC leader, who is known as one of the heavy weights in politics in Edo State, comments on other national issues which includes, but is not limited to, the politics of the First and Second Republics.

Original_Search_Title: Mugabe's Finger Will Be Free And Fair
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mugabe's Finger Will Be Free And Fair
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — IF YOU have been able to make sense of what is being said about tomorrow's elections in Zimbabwe, you deserve to win the lottery this weekend.
I confess that I lack the intellectual tools to analyse an election that is not going to be free and fair.
