Particularly hard hit were banana producers in the south of the country. Although the infestation was only reported from Niassa and Manica provinces, in the north and centre of the country, the South African ban covered all of Mozanmbique.

Original_Search_Title: SA 2010 Final Battle - Eagles Know Foes Today
Headline: Nigeria: SA 2010 Final Battle - Eagles Know Foes Today
Body_Text:
Lagos — The outcome of a simple ceremony lasting barely 45 minutes and taking place in the hallowed chambers of the auditorium of the FIFA headquarters in Zurich will go a long way in determining the World Cup qualification dreams of millions of Nigerians and others across the African continent.
For the ceremony billed to kick off at 11am (Nig time) is the draw for the final round of qualifying for the World Cup taking place in South Africa in 2010.

Original_Search_Title: No Logic in Preaching Development to the Sick
Headline: Uganda: No Logic in Preaching Development to the Sick
Body_Text:
President Museveni's speech at Uganda's 46th Independence Anniversary contained a lot of promise for the country especially on the side of economic growth. I was particularly touched by his reference to the expected oil boom and his advice to Ugandans not to fall sick so that they may enjoy the coming prosperity.
I am not sure what sickness he was talking about though, but what I know is that our leadership and many of our people are already afflicted with or affected by such diseases as corruption and moral decay. How will the latter who are already dying and who lack the wherewithal to keep themselves alive survive to enjoy the coming 'prosperity'?

Original_Search_Title: The End of the World?
Headline: Rwanda: The End of the World?
Body_Text:
The heavy rains and winds that hit some parts of the country recently, have caused a lost of damage but made no victims. Yet in Kirehe, in Eastern Province, some residents thought the end of the world had come.
It was just after lunch, at around 2 pm. Yvonne Uwamariya was bathing the kids to prepare them for their afternoon nap. Suddenly, the weather changed.

Original_Search_Title: HIPC Initiative - the Economic Hall of Shame
Headline: Cameroon: HIPC Initiative - the Economic Hall of Shame
Body_Text:
If you want to know whether a country is enjoying economic growth, go to the suburbs.
Without downplaying the important aspect of the economic variables that matter when we talk about the growth rate of the economy of every nation, emphasis must be placed on how data is gathered, processed and analysed, before being provided for publication or put at the disposal of Bretton Wood's financial tourists.

Original_Search_Title: ANC Picks Deputy Leader Motlanthe As Interim President
Headline: South Africa: ANC Picks Deputy Leader Motlanthe As Interim President
Body_Text:
Lagos — Following the resignation of South African Pres-ident Thabo Mbeki, African National Con-gress (ANC) leader Jacob Zuma said yesterday that his deputy in the ANC, Kgalema Motlanthe, would become the country's interim president.
Zuma said the decision would be formally announced when parliament meets - on Thursday - to vote on the appointment.

Original_Search_Title: Govt Told to Aid Science Teachers
Headline: Uganda: Govt Told to Aid Science Teachers
Body_Text:
Kampala — TEACHERS of sciences should be highly motivated so as to promote technological innovations in the country, prominent scientists and researchers advised yesterday.
The scientists, who made their appeal at the National Science Week 2008 conference that opened at Serena Hotel in Kampala, asked the Government to speed up the formulation of the National Policy on Science and Technology.

Original_Search_Title: Small Fruit Processing Machines On Display
Headline: Mozambique: Small Fruit Processing Machines On Display
Body_Text:
Maputo — Every year enormous quantities of fruit rot in the Mozambican countryside, for lack of processing facilities - but some businesses believe they can halt this waste.
At last week's 44th edition of the Maputo International Trade Fair (FACIM), the company Pro-Campo showed that giant factories are not needed. It is quite possible to process fruit using small machines that can easily be transported.

Original_Search_Title: Gacaca - 15 People Fined About $3,000 Each for Looting During Genocide
Headline: Rwanda: Gacaca - 15 People Fined About $3,000 Each for Looting During Genocide
Body_Text:
Kigali — Fifteen people have been fined in a single trial by the semi-traditional Gacaca Court of Cyivugiza Cell of Nyamirambo Sector, Kigali, to each pay within 15 days 1.5 million Rwandan Francs (2, 726 USD) for goods looted during the 1994 genocide.
They were also found guilty of destruction of seven houses and banana fields belonging to the Bihayihayi family in the Gatare Cell. The value of looted and destroyed goods were estimated at 27,584, 000 Rwandan Francs (50, 000 USD), according to the court.

Original_Search_Title: Sh28 Billion Released for SACCOs
Headline: Uganda: Sh28 Billion Released for SACCOs
Body_Text:
Kampala — Sh28b is available for distribution to Savings and Credit Co-operative Societies (SACCOs) and individuals who have good commercial projects.
"I talked to the minister of micro-finance, Salim Saleh, today and he confirmed that the money is available," Museveni told a rally at Esingu playground near Kaberamaido district headquarters on Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Gathering Storm of Expectations in Nairobi Slum
Headline: Kenya: Gathering Storm of Expectations in Nairobi Slum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — For the first time in its 60 years of existence, there is a ray of hope for the one million inhabitants of Kibera, one of the world's most densely-populated slums.
After spending most of his life on opposition benches - or in prison - as a champion of the poor, the member of parliament for this desperately poor constituency is now the prime minister of Kenya.

Original_Search_Title: An Overspeeding Bus Almost Claimed My Life
Headline: Uganda: An Overspeeding Bus Almost Claimed My Life
Body_Text:
He had taken medical supplies to his very ill 96-year-old father in Kisoro. On his return journey, he boarded a Gateway bus at 4.30a.m. Eugene Zeen tells Harriet S. Okot what it means to be a survivor.
Eugene Zeen lived to tell about his narrow escape after an accident that claimed seven lives and left 53 injured.

Original_Search_Title: Lawal Warns Dream Team IV On Team Spirit
Headline: Nigeria: Lawal Warns Dream Team IV On Team Spirit
Body_Text:
Former International, Garba Lawal has urged Nigerian Olympic Eagles' squad to the forthcoming Beijing Olympic to keep their heads together and health if they are to go all the way and win the gold medal.
Lawal, a member of the original Dream Team that won gold medal at the quadrennial multi-sports event in Atlanta, 1996, told KickOffNigeria.com that apart from the talent in the squad, team spirit and luck would won Nigeria the gold medal.

Original_Search_Title: Why We Couldn't Probe Obasanjo -Masari
Headline: Nigeria: Why We Couldn't Probe Obasanjo -Masari
Body_Text:
Musa Simon Reef & Isiaka Wakili — Speaker Aminu Bello Masari headed the House of Representatives from 2003 - 2007. In this interview with Sunday Trust, he spoke on the current probes of some key sectors by the National Assembly, saying he is not suprised by the revelations from these probes. He is worried about the the lack of internal democracy in the PDP and how he survived impeachment plots as Speaker, among other issues. Excerpts
What can you say about all the probes barked upon by the current House of Representatives?

Original_Search_Title: Losses Reveal Cost Pressures On Tea Companies
Headline: Kenya: Losses Reveal Cost Pressures On Tea Companies
Body_Text:
The tea industry could be headed for a fresh round of restructuring in operations, aimed at cutting down on cost, as two more firms reported losses over last year.
Listed firms Williamson Tea Kenya Limited and Kapchorua Tea Company Limited joined the growing list of loss-makers in the year-ended March 2008 with Sh73.4 million and Sh23.1 million losses respectively due to depressed pricing of the commodity in the international markets. They are also blaming the rising wage bill, high inflation and a fluctuating currency.

Original_Search_Title: Is Museveni's Claim On Food Prices Logical?
Headline: Uganda: Is Museveni's Claim On Food Prices Logical?
Body_Text:
President George Salongo Bush has recently given explanation that the main cause of the current food crisis is high consumption in China and India. Remember every four people you meet in the world, one of them is a Chinese. Similarly, every six people you meet in the World one of them is an Indian.
However, despite this scenario, the Chinese or Indian population numbers have not just increased today. China and India have had high populations for a long time. So how come they have all of a sudden decided to eat all the food available in the world now and not before?

Original_Search_Title: Alternatives to the Food Crisis
Headline: Africa: Alternatives to the Food Crisis
Body_Text:
In last week's Pambazuka News, Ian Angus looked at the causes of the food crisis. This week, he argues that alternatives to the food crisis must by their very nature be informed by alternatives to global capitalism.
"Nowhere in the world, in no act of genocide, in no war, are so many people killed per minute, per hour and per day as those who are killed by hunger and poverty on our planet."

Original_Search_Title: Govt Sets Prices for Commodities
Headline: Uganda: Govt Sets Prices for Commodities
Body_Text:
Kampala — In a bid to fight procurement corruption in public offices, the government has issued prices for common consumer goods that are clearly at odds with the surging market rates.
At the end of 2007, the Public Procurement and Disposal of Public Assets Authority (PPDA) compiled a 74-page list of common user items and average prices for suppliers and services, which it has now issued to accounting officers and those involved with tendering and purchases.

Original_Search_Title: The Final Chapter of a Little Twin Girl
Headline: Uganda: The Final Chapter of a Little Twin Girl
Body_Text:
Kampala — MARIAM Nakato and Halima Babirye were to celebrate their 10th birthday in August. But the fire that struck Budo Junior on April 14, killing 20 nine to 10-year-old girls including Nakato, denied the twins the joint celebrations which they had enjoyed for the past nine years.
The primary four pupils perished when Nassolo dormitory went up in flames at about 9:30pm.

Original_Search_Title: Mechanisation Buoys BAK Storage
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mechanisation Buoys BAK Storage
Body_Text:
Harare — BAK Storage is currently benefiting from the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe mechanisation programme and non-governmental organisation contracts, TSL managing director Mr Richard Musvaire said in a trading update.
Mr Musvaire said there had been business from the central bank's programme but the business model at the division had been "altered because there had been a drop in volumes from the traditional customers Unilever, NatFoods and Victoria Foods".

Original_Search_Title: Mwenje - the Lawmaker Who Almost Always Broke the Law
Headline: Kenya: Mwenje - the Lawmaker Who Almost Always Broke the Law
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Politics for the combative former Embakasi MP, the late David Mwenje, did not come easy, as he would confess.
During a trip to Rwanda he confessed how he once threatened a former Kanu national organising secretary, now a senior Government official, with a gun to get party nomination clearance for elections.

Original_Search_Title: Higher Education - Market Versus Reality
Headline: Kenya: Higher Education - Market Versus Reality
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In the last 20 years, the Kenyan higher education system has expanded by leaps and bounds. This expansion has been lopsided in favour of social sciences. It is the reality the new Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education graduates are meeting.
Education and other policy makers know social sciences have less strategic value, locally and globally, compared with "hard sciences". No one will say that too loudly, too many people may get annoyed.

Original_Search_Title: Appeals Court Increases Genocide Priest's Term to Life
Headline: Rwanda: Appeals Court Increases Genocide Priest's Term to Life
Body_Text:
The International Criminal Tribunal for Rwanda unanimously quashed a Catholic priest's sentence of 15 years imprisonment and instead handed him life in prison.
The Court on Wednesday overturned the conviction of Fr Athanase Seromba for aiding and abetting genocide and extermination as a crime against humanity and substituted it with conviction for committing genocide and extermination as a crime against humanity.

Original_Search_Title: Kuyateh Shines for Shine Africa
Headline: Gambia: Kuyateh Shines for Shine Africa
Body_Text:
Shine Africa, a UK registered charity that has been helping to support nursery education, with a mission to share knowledge skills and resources to benefit the children of the Gambia, are back having geared up for more charity works and activities this year.
Speaking to the Daily Observer in an exclusive interview, Ian and Slynn Jones declared the year 2007 a success in charity works and announced that they are ready and poised for more this year.

Original_Search_Title: Commissioner Responds to Diamond Fracas
Headline: Namibia: Commissioner Responds to Diamond Fracas
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Diamond Commissioner in the Ministry of Mines and Energy (MME), Kennedy Hamutenya, yesterday denounced suggestions by Nambib Resources Managing Director, Dr Subramaniam Ragubathi that diamond inspectors and diamond police officers were involved in foul play in the ongoing saga between Nambib and Bret Investments over the Toscanini diamonds.
He said that under no circumstances were the diamond inspectors and police involved in an illegal or ultra vires manner with respect of the handling, transportation and valuation of diamonds from Toscanini along the Skeleton Coast to Block 3 the Orange River.

Original_Search_Title: Individual EPAs 'Do Not' Undermine Regional Integration
Headline: Central Africa: Individual EPAs 'Do Not' Undermine Regional Integration
Body_Text:
Brussels — Central African countries have committed themselves to finalising an economic partnership agreement (EPA) with the European Union by June this year.
During recent discussions in Douala, Cameroon, representatives of Central African governments and the EU's executive, the European Commission, approved a "roadmap" for further talks. Its main objective was that a comprehensive trade liberalisation deal should be reached within the next four months.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Woos FDC Youth to NRM
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Woos FDC Youth to NRM
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has vowed to bring the youth who have joined the opposition Forum for Democratic Change (FDC) party back to the National Resistance Movement (NRM).
"We shall bring them back from FDC because they do not know what they are doing," the President said at Mugonya Primary School in Bushenyi on Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Kagame Tells His Own Story
Headline: Rwanda: Kagame Tells His Own Story
Body_Text:
Paul Kagame almost didn't make it to Rwanda to lead the RPF after the death of Fred Rwigyema. It was through a combination of extraordinary resourcefulness, intelligence, guile, some well-placed connections and luck that he did.
Yet Kagame, according to childhood friends, was a measured person. He didn't react quickly. He wasn't quick to get involved or embroiled.

Original_Search_Title: Sparks Fly as MPs Debate Power Crisis
Headline: South Africa: Sparks Fly as MPs Debate Power Crisis
Body_Text:
Cape Town — Most opposition parties called yesterday for heads to roll over the national electricity crisis during a special sitting of Parliament to debate the matter.
But their finger pointing was rebuffed by Public Enterprises Minister Alec Erwin, who said what was needed was solutions, not "baying for sacrificial lambs".

Original_Search_Title: Party Talks - Compromise a Necessary Evil
Headline: Zimbabwe: Party Talks - Compromise a Necessary Evil
Body_Text:
Harare — IN 1997, I decided to pass up a chance to attain the status of being 'one of the founding members' of the Movement for Democratic Change (MDC). Since then I have never joined any other political party up to today. I had several reasons for all this.
I intend to highlight them in my forthcoming book on my role and contribution to the student movement in Zimbabwe. But for the purposes of this debate, I will immediately highlight one of them.

Original_Search_Title: Buganda Premier Gets Instrument of Power
Headline: Uganda: Buganda Premier Gets Instrument of Power
Body_Text:
Kampala — HUNDREDS of people yesterday thronged the entrance of the Mengo palace to witness the handing over of the Buganda kingdom mace (Ddamula) to the new Katikkiro (premier), John Baptist Walusimbi.
Kabaka Ronald Muwenda Mutebi, clad in a light cream tunic, navy blue jacket and black shoes, performed the brief ceremony of handing over the mace to Walusimbi, 64.

Original_Search_Title: A Home At Journey's End
Headline: Uganda: A Home At Journey's End
Body_Text:
Kampala — A country without proper policies to take care of its elderly may need more homes like St. Paul's Family Centre in Mbarara, writes Stephen Ssenkaaba
Ova wa (where are you from?)" the old man, who is smartly dressed in a white Kaunda suit, asks me after catching me staring at him. It is a bright Saturday morning and I am visiting St. Paul's Family Centre for the Aged, a home for the elderly in Mbarara District.
